---
title: "Ligand–Receptor Recruitment Along the Classical Niche Ablation Response Trajectory"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    toc_depth: 3
    number_sections: true
date: "2026-04-22"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE,
                      fig.width = 10, fig.height = 5)
```

```{r libraries}
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(anndata)
  library(Matrix)
  library(dplyr)
  library(tidyr)
  library(ggplot2)
  library(ggthemes)
  library(RColorBrewer)
  library(patchwork)
  library(here)
})

# Source helper functions
source("basal_ablation/src/xenium_utils/pl/lr_heatmap_helpers.R")
RETICULATE_PYTHON='.conda/envs/xenium-rapids/bin/python'


CELLCHAT_PATH <- "ref_data/Mus_musculus/rl/CellChatDB.mouse.v2.rda"
H5AD_PATH     <- "basal_ablation/data/processed/xenium/niche_trajectory_classical_pruned_d24_filterv2_d1rm/st_data_classical_niche_DC_env.h5ad"
FIG_DIR       <- "basal_ablation/results/figures/chemokine_dc1/"

dir.create(FIG_DIR, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

rose_theme <- theme(
  axis.text  = element_text(size = 13, color = "black"),
  axis.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold")
)

X_LAB   <- "Classical niche ablation response (DC1)"
MIN_PCT           <- 0.009   # min fraction cells expressing ligand within lineage
MIN_LIG_CELLS     <- 500     # min cells expressing ligand within lineage (absolute count)
# Ligand passes filter if EITHER condition is met (OR logic)
MIN_REC_FRAC      <- 0.01    # min fraction receptor+ cells within lineage (overall, not per-bin)

# Cell-annotation level filters (cell type mode) - more stringent than lineage
MIN_PCT_CELLTYPE       <- 0.02    # min fraction cells expressing ligand within cell type
MIN_LIG_CELLS_CELLTYPE <- 300     # min cells expressing ligand within cell type
MIN_REC_FRAC_CELLTYPE  <- 0.02    # min receptor+ fraction within cell type

# Chemokine gene lists — for annotation/filtering of results, not for restricting tested pairs
CHEMOKINE_LIGANDS   <- c("Ccl2", "Ccl5", "Ccl17",
                          "Cxcl9", "Cxcl10", "Cxcl12", "Cxcl13", "Cxcl16", "Mif", 'Ccl9', 'Cxcl1')
CHEMOKINE_RECEPTORS <- c("Ccr2", "Ccr4", "Ccr6", "Ccr7",
                          "Cx3cr1", "Cxcr3", "Cxcr4", "Cxcr5", "Cxcr6", "Xcr1")

# Type I IFN ligands — sparse but important; use relaxed filtering
IFN_I_LIGANDS <- c("Ifna2", "Ifna13", "Ifne", "Ifnb1")
MIN_IFN_CELLS <- 30  # relaxed threshold for IFN-I ligands

LINEAGE_MAP <- c(
  Classical = 'Cancer', 
  Basal = 'Cancer', 
  Mesenchymal = 'Cancer', 
  # Cancer
  Cancer_epithelial              = "Cancer",
  `Cancer_epithelial_neuronal-like` = "Cancer",
  Cancer_mesenchymal             = "Cancer",
  # Myeloid
  Macrophage             = "Mac_Mono",
  Monocyte               = "Mac_Mono",
  cDC1                   = "DC",
  cDC2                   = "DC",
  mregDC                 = "DC",
  pDC                    = "pDC",
  Neutrophil             = "Neutrophil",
  Granulocyte            = "Neutrophil",
  # Stromal
  Fibroblast             = "Fibroblast",
  `Mesothelial/apCAF` = 'Fibroblast',
  PSC                    = "Fibroblast",
  `Mesenchymal-like_stroma` = "Stroma",
  Mesothelial            = "Stroma",
  Mural                  = "Stroma",
  Smooth_muscle          = "Stroma",
  Adipocyte              = "Stroma",
  Pericyte = 'Stroma',
  # Lymphoid
  CD8_T                  = "T_NK_ILC",
  Tconv                  = "T_NK_ILC",
  NK                     = "T_NK_ILC",
  Treg                   = "T_NK_ILC",
  gdT                    = "T_NK_ILC",
  gdT_CD8                = "T_NK_ILC",
  MAIT                   = "T_NK_ILC",
  `Innate-like_T`        = "T_NK_ILC",
  ILC1                   = "T_NK_ILC",
  ILC2                   = "T_NK_ILC",
  ILC3                   = "T_NK_ILC",
  NK_ILC1 = 'T_NK_ILC', 
  Lymphoid_proliferating = "T_NK_ILC",
  B_cell                 = "B_Plasma",
  Plasma_cell = 'B_Plasma', 
  Plasmablast            = "B_Plasma",
  # Endothelial
  Endothelial_vascular   = "Endothelial",
  Endothelial_lymphatic  = "Endothelial",
  # Epithelial (normal)
  Acinar                 = "Epithelial",
  ADM                    = "Epithelial",
  Ductal                 = "Epithelial",
  Islet                  = "Epithelial",
  `Tuft-like`            = "Epithelial",
  # Neural
  Neuron                 = "Neural",
  Schwann_cell           = "Neural"
)


# Cell types for cell type mode (cell_annotation level) - populated after data loading
SENDER_CELLTYPES   <- NULL
RECEIVER_CELLTYPES <- NULL

DC1_N_BINS = 30

set.seed(42)
```

---

# Introduction

This notebook tests ligand → receptor recruitment relationships along the **classical niche ablation response trajectory** (DC\_1\_rank\_flip). This axis captures how the tumor microenvironment of the classical PDAC niche evolves in response to basal cell ablation: DC1 ≈ 0 corresponds to the untreated classical niche; DC1 ≈ 1 corresponds to the late ablation-induced immune response. 

**Statistical approach:** Tier1 (lm) and tier2 (local niche correlation) used to identify LR relationships. Environment cells are assigned DC1 bins matching cancer cell definitions, and analysis is restricted to bins 0-27. This is tested at the cell type and total niche level. 

**LR pairs tested:** All CellChatDB pairs where both the ligand and at least one receptor subunit gene are present in the 480-gene Xenium panel. Chemokine-specific pairs are annotated in results but all pairs are tested.

---

# Data Loading

```{r load_adata}
adata  <- read_h5ad(H5AD_PATH)
obs_df <- adata$obs
# set a column that will indicate if a niche was part of the original trajectory
obs_df$in_trajectory = !(is.na(obs_df$DC_1_rank))

cat("Loaded:", nrow(obs_df), "cells,", ncol(adata$X), "genes\n")
```

```{r build_lr_pairs}
e_cc   <- new.env()
load(CELLCHAT_PATH, envir = e_cc)
cc_int <- e_cc$CellChatDB.mouse$interaction

# For each interaction, find the first receptor subunit gene present in the panel
cc_int$receptor_gene <- sapply(cc_int$receptor.symbol, function(x) {
  syms <- trimws(unlist(strsplit(x, ",")))
  hit  <- syms[syms %in% adata$var_names]
  if (length(hit) == 0) NA_character_ else hit[1]
})

LR_PAIRS <- cc_int[
  (cc_int$ligand %in% adata$var_names | cc_int$receptor_gene %in% adata$var_names) &
  !is.na(cc_int$receptor_gene),
  c("ligand", "receptor", "receptor.symbol", "receptor_gene", "pathway_name")
]
LR_PAIRS <- LR_PAIRS[!duplicated(paste(LR_PAIRS$ligand, LR_PAIRS$receptor_gene)), ]
rownames(LR_PAIRS) <- NULL

# Annotate chemokine pairs
LR_PAIRS$is_chemokine <- LR_PAIRS$ligand %in% CHEMOKINE_LIGANDS &
                          LR_PAIRS$receptor_gene %in% CHEMOKINE_RECEPTORS
# Expanded: any pair with a chemokine ligand (regardless of receptor)
LR_PAIRS$is_chemokine_expanded <- LR_PAIRS$ligand %in% CHEMOKINE_LIGANDS

cat("LR pairs to test:", nrow(LR_PAIRS), "\n")
cat("  of which chemokine LR:", sum(LR_PAIRS$is_chemokine), "\n")
cat("  of which chemokine expanded:", sum(LR_PAIRS$is_chemokine_expanded), "\n")
print(LR_PAIRS[LR_PAIRS$is_chemokine, c("ligand", "receptor_gene", "pathway_name")])
```


## Load cytokine annotations

```{r load_cytokine_anno}
library(readxl)

SIGNAL_ANNO_PATH <- "basal_ablation/data/features/xenium_panel/44CXV9_mPancreas_480g_gene_list_annotated.xlsx"

# Helper function to convert GMT-like format to named list
# GMT format: col1 = program name, col2 = description (ignored), col3+ = genes
gmt_to_list <- function(df) {
  result <- list()
  for (i in seq_len(nrow(df))) {
    program_name <- as.character(df[i, 1])
    # Get genes from column 3 onwards, removing NAs
    genes <- as.character(df[i, 3:ncol(df)])
    genes <- genes[!is.na(genes) & genes != ""]
    result[[program_name]] <- genes
  }
  result
}

signal_anno <- read_excel(SIGNAL_ANNO_PATH, sheet = "signal_anno_broad", col_names = FALSE)
gene_anno <- read_excel(SIGNAL_ANNO_PATH, sheet = "44CXV9_mPancreas_480g_gene_list", col_names = T)

# Convert GMT format to named list of gene sets
cytokine_signatures <- gmt_to_list(signal_anno)
cat("Loaded", length(cytokine_signatures), "cytokine programs\n")
for (prog in names(cytokine_signatures)) {
  cat("  ", prog, ":", length(cytokine_signatures[[prog]]), "genes\n")
}

panel_cyto_genes = cc_int %>% dplyr::filter(ligand.symbol %in% gene_anno$Gene) %>% .$ligand.symbol %>% unique()

# Add Ifn_I pseudo-gene (summed IFN-I ligands) and individual IFN-I ligands to panel_cyto_genes
panel_cyto_genes <- c(panel_cyto_genes, "Ifn_I", IFN_I_LIGANDS)
panel_cyto_genes <- unique(panel_cyto_genes)
cat("Panel cytokine genes:", length(panel_cyto_genes), "(includes Ifn_I pseudo-gene and IFN-I ligands)\n")
```

---

# Data Preparation

```{r impute_dc1}
obs_df$DC_1_rank = ifelse(is.na(obs_df$DC_1_rank), obs_df$DC_1_rank_env, obs_df$DC_1_rank)
sum(is.na(obs_df$DC_1_rank))
```

```{r extract_genes}
# Extract all ligand and receptor genes in LR_PAIRS
all_lr_genes   <- unique(c(LR_PAIRS$ligand, LR_PAIRS$receptor_gene, unique(unlist(cytokine_signatures))))
genes_in_panel <- intersect(all_lr_genes, adata$var_names)
cat("LR genes in panel:", length(genes_in_panel), "/", length(all_lr_genes), "\n")

# Raw counts (for receptor thresholding)
X        <- adata$layers[["counts_segger"]] %>% as.matrix()
gene_idx <- match(genes_in_panel, adata$var_names)
for (i in seq_along(genes_in_panel))
  obs_df[[genes_in_panel[i]]] <- as.numeric(X[, gene_idx[i]])

# Log1p normalized expression (for ligand sender analysis)
X_norm   <- adata$layers[["log1p_norm"]] %>% as.matrix()
all_lr_ligands_panel <- intersect(unique(LR_PAIRS$ligand), adata$var_names)
lig_idx <- match(all_lr_ligands_panel, adata$var_names)
for (i in seq_along(all_lr_ligands_panel))
  obs_df[[paste0(all_lr_ligands_panel[i], "_norm")]] <- as.numeric(X_norm[, lig_idx[i]])

obs_df$lib_size     <- rowSums(X)
obs_df$log_lib_size <- log1p(obs_df$lib_size)
obs_df$cell_int_idx <- as.character(seq_len(nrow(obs_df)) - 1L)
obs_df$lineage      <- LINEAGE_MAP[as.character(obs_df$cell_annotation)]

# Set cell types for cell type mode analysis (cell_annotation level)
# Filter to cell types with at least MIN_CELLS_CELLTYPE cells
MIN_CELLS_CELLTYPE <- 200
ct_counts <- table(obs_df$cell_annotation[!is.na(obs_df$cell_annotation)])
SENDER_CELLTYPES   <- names(ct_counts[ct_counts >= MIN_CELLS_CELLTYPE])
RECEIVER_CELLTYPES <- SENDER_CELLTYPES
cat("Cell types for cell type mode:", length(SENDER_CELLTYPES), "of", length(ct_counts),
    "(filtered to n >=", MIN_CELLS_CELLTYPE, ")\n")

# check for NA lineage conversion
table(obs_df$lineage, useNA = 'always')
```



```{r dc1_trim}
DC1_N_BINS    <- 30L
DC1_TRIM_LOW  <- 0L
DC1_TRIM_HIGH <- 2L

obs_df$DC1_bin30 <- cut(obs_df$DC_1_rank, breaks = DC1_N_BINS, labels = FALSE)
bin_breaks <- seq(min(obs_df$DC_1_rank, na.rm = TRUE),
                  max(obs_df$DC_1_rank, na.rm = TRUE),
                  length.out = DC1_N_BINS + 1)
bin_lines  <- bin_breaks[(DC1_TRIM_LOW + 1):(DC1_N_BINS - DC1_TRIM_HIGH)]

obs_df <- obs_df[
  !is.na(obs_df$DC1_bin30) &
  obs_df$DC1_bin30 > DC1_TRIM_LOW &
  obs_df$DC1_bin30 <= (DC1_N_BINS - DC1_TRIM_HIGH), ]

DC1_T_MIN <- min(obs_df$DC_1_rank, na.rm = TRUE)
DC1_T_MAX <- max(obs_df$DC_1_rank, na.rm = TRUE)

cat("Cells after trimming:", nrow(obs_df), "\n")
cat("DC1 range:", round(DC1_T_MIN, 3), "–", round(DC1_T_MAX, 3), "\n")
print(sort(table(obs_df$lineage), decreasing = TRUE))
```

What are the cells per bin?

```{r}
table(obs_df %>% dplyr::filter(in_trajectory) %>% .$DC1_bin30) %>% unique()
```


## Precompute niche receptor counts

```{r niche_counts}
conn   <- adata$obsp[["mingle_connectivities"]]
conn@x <- rep(1.0, length(conn@x))

keep_idx  <- as.integer(obs_df$cell_int_idx) + 1L
conn_trim <- conn[keep_idx, keep_idx]

obs_df$n_niche_total <- as.numeric(conn_trim %*% rep(1.0, nrow(obs_df)))

# Precompute for all LR receptor genes × receiver lineages
all_lr_receptors <- intersect(unique(LR_PAIRS$receptor_gene), genes_in_panel)

# --- VECTORIZED: Lineage niche counts (one matrix multiplication) ---
lineage_indicators <- sapply(RECEIVER_LINEAGES, function(lin) {
  as.numeric(!is.na(obs_df$lineage) & obs_df$lineage == lin)
})
niche_lineage_counts <- as.matrix(conn_trim %*% lineage_indicators)
for (i in seq_along(RECEIVER_LINEAGES)) {
  obs_df[[paste0("n_", RECEIVER_LINEAGES[i], "_niche")]] <- niche_lineage_counts[, i]
}

# --- VECTORIZED: Receptor-positive × lineage counts (one matrix multiplication) ---
combo_list <- list()
combo_names <- character(0)
for (lin in RECEIVER_LINEAGES) {
  is_lin <- !is.na(obs_df$lineage) & obs_df$lineage == lin
  for (rec in all_lr_receptors) {
    nm <- paste0("n_", rec, "_", lin)
    combo_names <- c(combo_names, nm)
    combo_list[[nm]] <- as.numeric(is_lin & obs_df[[rec]] > 1)
  }
}
indicator_mat <- do.call(cbind, combo_list)
niche_rec_counts <- as.matrix(conn_trim %*% indicator_mat)
for (i in seq_along(combo_names)) {
  obs_df[[combo_names[i]]] <- niche_rec_counts[, i]
}

cat("Niche counts computed for", length(RECEIVER_LINEAGES), "receiver lineages,",
    length(all_lr_receptors), "receptor genes\n")
```

## Compute niche cell type counts

```{r niche_celltype_counts}
# --- Cell-type level niche counts (for cell type mode) ---
celltype_indicators <- sapply(SENDER_CELLTYPES, function(ct) {
  as.numeric(!is.na(obs_df$cell_annotation) & obs_df$cell_annotation == ct)
})
niche_celltype_counts <- as.matrix(conn_trim %*% celltype_indicators)
for (i in seq_along(SENDER_CELLTYPES)) {
  obs_df[[paste0("n_", SENDER_CELLTYPES[i], "_niche_ct")]] <- niche_celltype_counts[, i]
}

# Receptor-positive × cell type counts
combo_list_ct <- list()
combo_names_ct <- character(0)
for (ct in RECEIVER_CELLTYPES) {
  is_ct <- !is.na(obs_df$cell_annotation) & obs_df$cell_annotation == ct
  for (rec in all_lr_receptors) {
    nm <- paste0("n_", rec, "_", ct, "_ct")
    combo_names_ct <- c(combo_names_ct, nm)
    combo_list_ct[[nm]] <- as.numeric(is_ct & obs_df[[rec]] > 1)
  }
}
indicator_mat_ct <- do.call(cbind, combo_list_ct)
niche_rec_counts_ct <- as.matrix(conn_trim %*% indicator_mat_ct)
for (i in seq_along(combo_names_ct)) {
  obs_df[[combo_names_ct[i]]] <- niche_rec_counts_ct[, i]
}

cat("Cell type niche counts computed for", length(SENDER_CELLTYPES), "cell types,",
    length(all_lr_receptors), "receptor genes\n")
```

## Compute niche-summed ligand (legacy - kept for niche mode)

```{r niche_ligand_compute}
cancer_mask <- obs_df$in_trajectory
cancer_pos  <- which(cancer_mask)

# Total transcript counts per retained cell (for normalisation)
total_counts     <- as.numeric(rowSums(X[keep_idx, ]))
niche_total_cnts <- as.numeric(conn_trim[cancer_pos, ] %*% total_counts)

# --- VECTORIZED: All ligands in one matrix multiplication ---
all_lr_ligands <- intersect(unique(LR_PAIRS$ligand), genes_in_panel)
ligand_mat <- sapply(all_lr_ligands, function(lig) as.numeric(obs_df[[lig]]))
niche_lig_sums <- as.matrix(conn_trim[cancer_pos, ] %*% ligand_mat)

for (i in seq_along(all_lr_ligands)) {
  obs_df[cancer_mask, paste0("niche_lig_", all_lr_ligands[i])] <-
    niche_lig_sums[, i] / pmax(niche_total_cnts, 1)
}

# Add Ifn_I pseudo-gene column (sum of IFN-I ligands)
ifn_genes_in_panel <- intersect(IFN_I_LIGANDS, names(obs_df))
if (length(ifn_genes_in_panel) > 0) {
  obs_df$Ifn_I <- rowSums(obs_df[, ifn_genes_in_panel, drop = FALSE], na.rm = TRUE)
  all_lr_ligands <- c(all_lr_ligands, "Ifn_I")
  cat("Added Ifn_I pseudo-gene to obs_df (sum of", paste(ifn_genes_in_panel, collapse = ", "), ")\n")
}

# Add Ifn_I pseudo-gene: sum of all IFN-I ligands in niche
ifn_genes_in_panel <- intersect(IFN_I_LIGANDS, names(obs_df))
if (length(ifn_genes_in_panel) > 0) {
  ifn_mat <- sapply(ifn_genes_in_panel, function(g) as.numeric(obs_df[[g]]))
  ifn_sum <- rowSums(ifn_mat, na.rm = TRUE)
  niche_ifn_sum <- as.numeric(conn_trim[cancer_pos, ] %*% ifn_sum)
  obs_df[cancer_mask, "niche_lig_Ifn_I"] <- niche_ifn_sum / pmax(niche_total_cnts, 1)
  cat("Added niche_lig_Ifn_I pseudo-gene (sum of", paste(ifn_genes_in_panel, collapse = ", "), ")\n")
}

cat("Niche ligand columns computed:", sum(grepl("^niche_lig_", names(obs_df))), "\n")
```

## Compute niche-summed receptor fraction (niche mode)

```{r niche_receptor_compute}
# For each cancer cell, compute the fraction of niche cells positive for each receptor
# This is: sum(receptor+ cells in niche) / sum(all cells in niche)
# Summed across ALL cell types (no lineage restriction)

# Total niche cells per cancer cell
niche_total_cells <- as.numeric(conn_trim[cancer_pos, ] %*% rep(1.0, nrow(obs_df)))

# Compute niche receptor+ fraction for each receptor (across all cell types)
for (rec in all_lr_receptors) {
  is_rec_pos <- obs_df[[rec]] > 1
  niche_rec_cnt <- as.numeric(conn_trim[cancer_pos, ] %*% as.numeric(is_rec_pos))
  col_name <- paste0("niche_rec_", rec)
  obs_df[cancer_mask, col_name] <- niche_rec_cnt / pmax(niche_total_cells, 1)
}
cat("Niche receptor columns computed:", sum(grepl("^niche_rec_", names(obs_df))), "\n")
```

---

# Bin-Level Aggregation

```{r bin_aggregate}
cancer_df <- obs_df[cancer_mask & !is.na(obs_df$DC1_bin30), ]

# Covariate means per bin (cancer cells)
cov_bins <- cancer_df %>%
  group_by(DC1_bin30) %>%
  summarize(
    n_cancer       = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Total niche cells per bin (across ALL lineages)
niche_totals_bin <- cancer_df %>%
  group_by(DC1_bin30) %>%
  summarize(
    n_niche_total_bin = sum(n_niche_total, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Receptor-positive counts per bin per lineage
rec_bins <- list()
for (rec in all_lr_receptors) {
  for (lin_r in RECEIVER_LINEAGES) {
    rec_col <- paste0("n_", rec, "_", lin_r)
    lin_col <- paste0("n_", lin_r, "_niche")
    if (!rec_col %in% names(cancer_df)) next

    tmp <- cancer_df %>%
      group_by(DC1_bin30) %>%
      summarize(
        n_rec_total  = sum(.data[[rec_col]], na.rm = TRUE),
        n_lin_total  = sum(.data[[lin_col]], na.rm = TRUE),
        .groups = "drop"
      ) %>%
      left_join(cov_bins, by = "DC1_bin30") %>%
      left_join(niche_totals_bin, by = "DC1_bin30")

    # Fraction within lineage per bin (for reference)
    tmp$rec_frac_within_lin <- tmp$n_rec_total / pmax(tmp$n_lin_total, 1)
    # Fraction of total niche (for modeling/plotting)
    tmp$rec_frac_bin <- tmp$n_rec_total / pmax(tmp$n_niche_total_bin, 1)
    # Overall fraction within lineage (summed across all bins, for filtering)
    tmp$rec_frac_within_lin_overall <- sum(tmp$n_rec_total, na.rm = TRUE) /
                                        pmax(sum(tmp$n_lin_total, na.rm = TRUE), 1)

    rec_bins[[paste(rec, lin_r, sep = "__")]] <- tmp
  }
}
# Filter: remove if overall receptor+ fraction within lineage is below threshold
rec_bins <- Filter(function(df) df$rec_frac_within_lin_overall[1] >= MIN_REC_FRAC, rec_bins)
cat("Receptor bin tables after filtering:", length(rec_bins), "\n")

# Sender expression per bin - pseudobulk: sum counts / total counts, then log1p
sender_bins <- list()
for (lin_s in SENDER_LINEAGES) {
  sub <- obs_df[!is.na(obs_df$lineage) & obs_df$lineage == lin_s &
                !is.na(obs_df$DC1_bin30), ]
  if (nrow(sub) < 10) next
  for (lig in all_lr_ligands) {
    lig_norm_col <- paste0(lig, "_norm")
    if (!lig %in% names(sub) || !lig_norm_col %in% names(sub)) next

    # Calculate expression stats within lineage
    lig_pct <- mean(sub[[lig]] > 0, na.rm = TRUE)
    lig_n_cells <- sum(sub[[lig]] > 0, na.rm = TRUE)

    # Filter: pass if EITHER pct >= MIN_PCT OR n_cells >= MIN_LIG_CELLS (OR logic)
    # IFN-I ligands use relaxed cell count threshold
    min_cells_thresh <- if (lig %in% IFN_I_LIGANDS) MIN_IFN_CELLS else MIN_LIG_CELLS
    if (lig_pct < MIN_PCT && lig_n_cells < min_cells_thresh) next

    tmp <- sub %>%
      group_by(DC1_bin30) %>%
      summarize(lig_expr_bin = log1p(sum(.data[[lig]], na.rm = TRUE) /
                                      pmax(sum(lib_size, na.rm = TRUE), 1)),
                n_sender     = n(),
                n_lig_cells  = sum(.data[[lig]] > 0, na.rm = TRUE),
                .groups = "drop")
    sender_bins[[paste(lin_s, lig, sep = "__")]] <- tmp
  }

  # Create Ifn_I pseudo-gene: sum of all IFN-I ligands
  ifn_genes_in_panel <- intersect(IFN_I_LIGANDS, names(sub))
  if (length(ifn_genes_in_panel) > 0) {
    # Sum raw counts across IFN-I ligands
    sub$Ifn_I_counts <- rowSums(sub[, ifn_genes_in_panel, drop = FALSE], na.rm = TRUE)
    n_ifn_cells_total <- sum(sub$Ifn_I_counts > 0, na.rm = TRUE)

    if (n_ifn_cells_total >= MIN_IFN_CELLS) {
      tmp_ifn <- sub %>%
        group_by(DC1_bin30) %>%
        summarize(lig_expr_bin = log1p(sum(Ifn_I_counts, na.rm = TRUE) /
                                        pmax(sum(lib_size, na.rm = TRUE), 1)),
                  n_sender     = n(),
                  n_lig_cells  = sum(Ifn_I_counts > 0, na.rm = TRUE),
                  .groups = "drop")
      sender_bins[[paste(lin_s, "Ifn_I", sep = "__")]] <- tmp_ifn
    }
  }
}
cat("Sender bin tables:", length(sender_bins), "\n")
cat("Ifn_I entries added:", sum(grepl("__Ifn_I$", names(sender_bins))), "\n")

# Niche-summed ligand per bin 
niche_lig_bins <- list()
for (lig in all_lr_ligands) {
  ncol_name <- paste0("niche_lig_", lig)
  if (!ncol_name %in% names(obs_df)) next
  tmp <- cancer_df %>%
    group_by(DC1_bin30) %>%
    summarize(niche_lig_bin = mean(.data[[ncol_name]], na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
    left_join(cov_bins, by = "DC1_bin30")
  # Keep only if at least one bin meets minimum niche ligand fraction
  if (!any(tmp$niche_lig_bin >= 0, na.rm = TRUE)) next # don't need to filter here
  niche_lig_bins[[lig]] <- tmp
}

# Add Ifn_I pseudo-gene to niche_lig_bins
if ("niche_lig_Ifn_I" %in% names(cancer_df)) {
  tmp_ifn <- cancer_df %>%
    group_by(DC1_bin30) %>%
    summarize(niche_lig_bin = mean(niche_lig_Ifn_I, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
    left_join(cov_bins, by = "DC1_bin30")
  niche_lig_bins[["Ifn_I"]] <- tmp_ifn
  cat("Added Ifn_I to niche_lig_bins\n")
}

cat("Niche ligand bin tables after filtering:", length(niche_lig_bins), "\n")

# Niche-summed receptor fraction per bin - across all cell types
niche_rec_bins <- list()
for (rec in all_lr_receptors) {
  col_name <- paste0("niche_rec_", rec)
  if (!col_name %in% names(cancer_df)) next
  tmp <- cancer_df %>%
    group_by(DC1_bin30) %>%
    summarize(niche_rec_bin = mean(.data[[col_name]], na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
    left_join(cov_bins, by = "DC1_bin30")
  # Keep only if at least one bin meets minimum receptor fraction
  if (!any(tmp$niche_rec_bin >= 0, na.rm = TRUE)) next
  niche_rec_bins[[rec]] <- tmp
}
cat("Niche receptor bin tables after filtering:", length(niche_rec_bins), "\n")

# --- Cell-type level sender bins ---
sender_celltype_bins <- list()
for (ct_s in SENDER_CELLTYPES) {
  sub <- obs_df[!is.na(obs_df$cell_annotation) & obs_df$cell_annotation == ct_s &
                !is.na(obs_df$DC1_bin30), ]
  if (nrow(sub) < 10) next
  for (lig in all_lr_ligands) {
    lig_norm_col <- paste0(lig, "_norm")
    if (!lig %in% names(sub) || !lig_norm_col %in% names(sub)) next

    lig_pct <- mean(sub[[lig]] > 0, na.rm = TRUE)
    lig_n_cells <- sum(sub[[lig]] > 0, na.rm = TRUE)

    # More stringent filtering for cell type level (OR logic)
    if (lig_pct < MIN_PCT_CELLTYPE && lig_n_cells < MIN_LIG_CELLS_CELLTYPE) next

    tmp <- sub %>%
      group_by(DC1_bin30) %>%
      summarize(lig_expr_bin = log1p(sum(.data[[lig]], na.rm = TRUE) /
                                      pmax(sum(lib_size, na.rm = TRUE), 1)),
                n_sender     = n(),
                n_lig_cells  = sum(.data[[lig]] > 0, na.rm = TRUE),
                .groups = "drop")
    sender_celltype_bins[[paste(ct_s, lig, sep = "__")]] <- tmp
  }

  # Create Ifn_I pseudo-gene: sum of all IFN-I ligands
  ifn_genes_in_panel <- intersect(IFN_I_LIGANDS, names(sub))
  if (length(ifn_genes_in_panel) > 0) {
    sub$Ifn_I_counts <- rowSums(sub[, ifn_genes_in_panel, drop = FALSE], na.rm = TRUE)
    n_ifn_cells_total <- sum(sub$Ifn_I_counts > 0, na.rm = TRUE)

    if (n_ifn_cells_total >= MIN_IFN_CELLS) {
      tmp_ifn <- sub %>%
        group_by(DC1_bin30) %>%
        summarize(lig_expr_bin = log1p(sum(Ifn_I_counts, na.rm = TRUE) /
                                        pmax(sum(lib_size, na.rm = TRUE), 1)),
                  n_sender     = n(),
                  n_lig_cells  = sum(Ifn_I_counts > 0, na.rm = TRUE),
                  .groups = "drop")
      sender_celltype_bins[[paste(ct_s, "Ifn_I", sep = "__")]] <- tmp_ifn
    }
  }
}
cat("Sender cell type bin tables:", length(sender_celltype_bins), "\n")
cat("Ifn_I entries added:", sum(grepl("__Ifn_I$", names(sender_celltype_bins))), "\n")

# --- Cell-type level receptor bins ---
rec_celltype_bins <- list()
for (rec in all_lr_receptors) {
  for (ct_r in RECEIVER_CELLTYPES) {
    rec_col <- paste0("n_", rec, "_", ct_r, "_ct")
    ct_col <- paste0("n_", ct_r, "_niche_ct")
    if (!rec_col %in% names(cancer_df)) next

    tmp <- cancer_df %>%
      group_by(DC1_bin30) %>%
      summarize(
        n_rec_total  = sum(.data[[rec_col]], na.rm = TRUE),
        n_ct_total   = sum(.data[[ct_col]], na.rm = TRUE),
        .groups = "drop"
      ) %>%
      left_join(cov_bins, by = "DC1_bin30") %>%
      left_join(niche_totals_bin, by = "DC1_bin30")

    tmp$rec_frac_within_ct <- tmp$n_rec_total / pmax(tmp$n_ct_total, 1)
    tmp$rec_frac_bin <- tmp$n_rec_total / pmax(tmp$n_niche_total_bin, 1)
    tmp$rec_frac_within_ct_overall <- sum(tmp$n_rec_total, na.rm = TRUE) /
                                       pmax(sum(tmp$n_ct_total, na.rm = TRUE), 1)

    rec_celltype_bins[[paste(rec, ct_r, sep = "__")]] <- tmp
  }
}
# More stringent filtering for cell type level
rec_celltype_bins <- Filter(function(df) df$rec_frac_within_ct_overall[1] >= MIN_REC_FRAC_CELLTYPE, rec_celltype_bins)
cat("Receptor cell type bin tables after filtering:", length(rec_celltype_bins), "\n")
```

## Filtering diagnostics

```{r filtering_diagnostics}
# Compute filtering metrics for all gene × cell_type combinations (vectorized)
compute_filtering_metrics <- function(df, genes, cell_types, col_name = "cell_annotation",
                                       threshold = 0) {
  # Filter to genes that exist in data

  genes <- intersect(genes, names(df))
  if (length(genes) == 0) return(data.frame())

  # Filter to valid cell types

  df <- df[!is.na(df[[col_name]]) & df[[col_name]] %in% cell_types, ]
  if (nrow(df) == 0) return(data.frame())


  # Compute n_total per cell type (single group_by)
  n_total_df <- df %>%
    group_by(cell_type = .data[[col_name]]) %>%
    summarize(n_total = n(), .groups = "drop")

 # Vectorized: compute expression stats for all genes at once
  # Convert gene columns to binary (expressing or not)
  expr_mat <- as.matrix(df[, genes, drop = FALSE]) > threshold

  # Sum per cell type using matrix multiplication with indicator
  ct_factor <- factor(df[[col_name]], levels = cell_types)
  ct_indicator <- model.matrix(~ ct_factor - 1)  # n_cells × n_celltypes indicator matrix
  colnames(ct_indicator) <- levels(ct_factor)

  # n_expressing = t(indicator) %*% expr_mat  → (n_celltypes × n_genes)
  n_expr_mat <- t(ct_indicator) %*% expr_mat

  # Convert to long format
  results <- expand.grid(cell_type = rownames(n_expr_mat), gene = colnames(n_expr_mat),
                          stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  results$n_expressing <- as.vector(n_expr_mat)

  # Join n_total

  results <- results %>%
    left_join(n_total_df, by = "cell_type") %>%
    mutate(pct_expressing = n_expressing / n_total) %>%
    filter(n_total > 0) %>%
    select(gene, cell_type, pct_expressing, n_expressing, n_total)

  results
}

# Ligand metrics (threshold = 0 to match sender_bins)
ligand_metrics <- compute_filtering_metrics(obs_df, all_lr_ligands, SENDER_CELLTYPES, threshold = 0)
if (nrow(ligand_metrics) > 0) {
  ligand_metrics$passes_pct <- ligand_metrics$pct_expressing >= MIN_PCT_CELLTYPE
  ligand_metrics$passes_count <- ligand_metrics$n_expressing >= MIN_LIG_CELLS_CELLTYPE
  ligand_metrics$passes_filter <- ligand_metrics$passes_pct | ligand_metrics$passes_count
  ligand_metrics$lineage <- LINEAGE_MAP[ligand_metrics$cell_type]
}

# Receptor metrics (threshold = 1 to match receptor positivity)
receptor_metrics <- compute_filtering_metrics(obs_df, all_lr_receptors, RECEIVER_CELLTYPES, threshold = 1)
if (nrow(receptor_metrics) > 0) {
  receptor_metrics$passes_filter <- receptor_metrics$pct_expressing >= MIN_REC_FRAC_CELLTYPE
  receptor_metrics$lineage <- LINEAGE_MAP[receptor_metrics$cell_type]
}

# Save for diagnostics
write.csv(ligand_metrics, file.path(FIG_DIR, "ligand_celltype_filtering_metrics.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(receptor_metrics, file.path(FIG_DIR, "receptor_celltype_filtering_metrics.csv"), row.names = FALSE)

cat("Ligand metrics:", nrow(ligand_metrics), "gene × cell_type combinations\n")
cat("  Passing filter:", sum(ligand_metrics$passes_filter), "\n")
cat("Receptor metrics:", nrow(receptor_metrics), "gene × cell_type combinations\n")
cat("  Passing filter:", sum(receptor_metrics$passes_filter), "\n")
```

## Cell type composition per DC1 bin

Compute fraction of each cell type (both `cell_annotation` and `cell_annotation_granular`) among all cells in each DC1 bin, then visualize as heatmaps. Code to make plot in figure 6D. 

```{r celltype_fractions_per_bin}
# Compute fractions for cell_annotation
ct_frac_coarse <- obs_df %>%
  filter(!is.na(DC1_bin30), !is.na(cell_annotation)) %>%
  group_by(DC1_bin30, cell_annotation) %>%
  summarize(n = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(DC1_bin30) %>%
  mutate(frac = n / sum(n)) %>%
  ungroup()

# Compute fractions for cell_annotation_granular
ct_frac_granular <- obs_df %>%
  filter(!is.na(DC1_bin30), !is.na(cell_annotation_granular)) %>%
  group_by(DC1_bin30, cell_annotation_granular) %>%
  summarize(n = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(DC1_bin30) %>%
  mutate(frac = n / sum(n)) %>%
  ungroup()

cat("Cell types (coarse):", length(unique(ct_frac_coarse$cell_annotation)), "\n")
cat("Cell types (granular):", length(unique(ct_frac_granular$cell_annotation_granular)), "\n")
```

```{r celltype_heatmap_coarse, fig.width=10, fig.height=8}
library(ComplexHeatmap)
library(circlize)

# exclude cell types that are less than 200 cells
cell_anno_counts = table(obs_df$cell_annotation) %>% sort()
filter_out_ct = names(cell_anno_counts)[cell_anno_counts < 200]
rm_ct = c("Mesenchymal-like_stroma", "Pericyte", "Mural" , "Smooth_muscle", 'Ductal', 'Tuft-like', 'Islet', 'ADM', 'Acinar', 'Endothelial_vascular', 'Endothelial_lymphatic')
filter_out_ct = unique(c(filter_out_ct, rm_ct))
# Pivot to matrix: rows = cell types, cols = DC1 bins
ct_mat_coarse <- ct_frac_coarse %>%
  dplyr::filter(!(cell_annotation %in% filter_out_ct)) %>%
  select(DC1_bin30, cell_annotation, frac) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = DC1_bin30, values_from = frac, values_fill = 0) %>%
  tibble::column_to_rownames("cell_annotation") %>%
  as.matrix()

# Order columns by bin number
ct_mat_coarse <- ct_mat_coarse[, order(as.numeric(colnames(ct_mat_coarse)))]

# Find position of bin 18 for vertical line
bin_cols <- as.numeric(colnames(ct_mat_coarse))
bin18_idx <- which(bin_cols == 18)

# Smooth rows: 3-bin rolling average (center + flanking neighbors)
smooth_rows <- function(mat) {
  n_cols <- ncol(mat)
  smoothed <- mat
  for (i in seq_len(nrow(mat))) {
    for (j in seq_len(n_cols)) {
      neighbors <- max(1, j-1):min(n_cols, j+1)
      smoothed[i, j] <- mean(mat[i, neighbors], na.rm = TRUE)
    }
  }
  smoothed
}
ct_mat_coarse_smooth <- smooth_rows(ct_mat_coarse)

# Z-score rows for visualization
zscore_rows <- function(mat) {
  t(apply(mat, 1, function(x) {
    if (sd(x, na.rm = TRUE) == 0) return(rep(0, length(x)))
    (x - mean(x, na.rm = TRUE)) / sd(x, na.rm = TRUE)
  }))
}
ct_mat_coarse_z <- zscore_rows(ct_mat_coarse_smooth)

# Order rows by bin where they reach maximum value (using smoothed values)
row_order <- order(apply(ct_mat_coarse_smooth, 1, which.max))

col_fun <- colorRamp2(c(-2, 0, 2), c("#4575b4", "white", "#d73027"))

ht_coarse <- Heatmap(
  ct_mat_coarse_z[row_order, , drop = FALSE],
  col = col_fun,
  name = "Z-score (coarse)",
  column_title = "Cell type composition shifts along DC1 (cell_annotation)",
  row_title = "Cell type",
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_columns = FALSE,
  show_row_names = TRUE,
  row_names_side = "left",
  row_names_gp = gpar(fontsize = 9),
  show_column_names = FALSE,
  rect_gp = gpar(col = "lightgrey", lwd = 0.3),
  na_col = "grey90",
  column_title_gp = gpar(fontsize = 12, fontface = "bold")
)

draw(ht_coarse)
decorate_heatmap_body("Z-score (coarse)", {
  x_pos <- bin18_idx / ncol(ct_mat_coarse_z)
  grid.lines(x = unit(x_pos, "npc"), y = unit(c(0, 1), "npc"), gp = gpar(col = "black", lwd = 2))
})

# Save
svg(file.path(FIG_DIR, "celltype_composition_dc1_coarse.svg"), width = 6, height = 8)
draw(ht_coarse)
decorate_heatmap_body("Z-score (coarse)", {
  x_pos <- bin18_idx / ncol(ct_mat_coarse_z)
  grid.lines(x = unit(x_pos, "npc"), y = unit(c(0, 1), "npc"), gp = gpar(col = "black", lwd = 2))
})
dev.off()
cat("Saved coarse cell type heatmap\n")
```

```{r celltype_heatmap_coarse_filtered, fig.width=10, fig.height=8}
# make another one that filters out some non-relevant populations

# exclude cell types that are less than 200 cells
cell_anno_counts = table(obs_df$cell_annotation) %>% sort()
filter_out_ct = names(cell_anno_counts)[cell_anno_counts < 200]
rm_ct = c("Mesenchymal-like_stroma", "Pericyte", "Mural" , "Smooth_muscle", 
          'Ductal', 'Tuft-like', 'Islet', 'ADM', 'Acinar', 'Endothelial_vascular', 'Endothelial_lymphatic', 
          'PSC', 'Fibroblast', 'Lymphoid_proliferating', 'Mesothelial/apCAF')
filter_out_ct = unique(c(filter_out_ct, rm_ct))
# Pivot to matrix: rows = cell types, cols = DC1 bins
ct_mat_coarse <- ct_frac_coarse %>%
  dplyr::filter(!(cell_annotation %in% filter_out_ct)) %>%
  select(DC1_bin30, cell_annotation, frac) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = DC1_bin30, values_from = frac, values_fill = 0) %>%
  tibble::column_to_rownames("cell_annotation") %>%
  as.matrix()

# Order columns by bin number
ct_mat_coarse <- ct_mat_coarse[, order(as.numeric(colnames(ct_mat_coarse)))]

# Find position of bin 18 for vertical line
bin_cols <- as.numeric(colnames(ct_mat_coarse))
bin18_idx <- which(bin_cols == 18)

# Smooth rows: 3-bin rolling average (center + flanking neighbors)
smooth_rows <- function(mat) {
  n_cols <- ncol(mat)
  smoothed <- mat
  for (i in seq_len(nrow(mat))) {
    for (j in seq_len(n_cols)) {
      neighbors <- max(1, j-1):min(n_cols, j+1)
      smoothed[i, j] <- mean(mat[i, neighbors], na.rm = TRUE)
    }
  }
  smoothed
}
ct_mat_coarse_smooth <- smooth_rows(ct_mat_coarse)

# Z-score rows for visualization
zscore_rows <- function(mat) {
  t(apply(mat, 1, function(x) {
    if (sd(x, na.rm = TRUE) == 0) return(rep(0, length(x)))
    (x - mean(x, na.rm = TRUE)) / sd(x, na.rm = TRUE)
  }))
}
ct_mat_coarse_z <- zscore_rows(ct_mat_coarse_smooth)

# Order rows by bin where they reach maximum value (using smoothed values)
row_order <- order(apply(ct_mat_coarse_smooth, 1, which.max))

col_fun <- colorRamp2(c(-2, 0, 2), c("#4575b4", "white", "#d73027"))

ht_coarse <- Heatmap(
  ct_mat_coarse_z[row_order, , drop = FALSE],
  col = col_fun,
  name = "Z-score (coarse)",
  column_title = "Cell type composition shifts along DC1",
  row_title = "Cell type",
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_columns = FALSE,
  show_row_names = TRUE,
  row_names_side = "left",
  row_names_gp = gpar(fontsize = 14),
  show_column_names = FALSE,
  #rect_gp = gpar(col = "lightgrey", lwd = 0.3),
  na_col = "grey90",
  column_title_gp = gpar(fontsize = 12, fontface = "bold")
)

draw(ht_coarse)
decorate_heatmap_body("Z-score (coarse)", {
  x_pos <- bin18_idx / ncol(ct_mat_coarse_z)
  grid.lines(x = unit(x_pos, "npc"), y = unit(c(0, 1), "npc"), gp = gpar(col = "black", lwd = 2))
})

# Save
svg(file.path(FIG_DIR, "celltype_composition_dc1_coarse_filtered.svg"), width = 4, height = 6)
draw(ht_coarse)
decorate_heatmap_body("Z-score (coarse)", {
  x_pos <- bin18_idx / ncol(ct_mat_coarse_z)
  grid.lines(x = unit(x_pos, "npc"), y = unit(c(0, 1), "npc"), gp = gpar(col = "black", lwd = 2))
})
dev.off()
cat("Saved coarse cell type heatmap\n")
```


```{r celltype_heatmap_granular, fig.width=12, fig.height=14}

# exclude cell types that are less than 200 cells
cell_anno_gran_counts = table(obs_df$cell_annotation_granular) %>% sort()
filter_out_ct = names(cell_anno_gran_counts)[cell_anno_gran_counts < 500]
rm_ct = names(cell_anno_gran_counts)[grepl("contam", names(cell_anno_gran_counts))]
#rm_ct = c("Mesenchymal-like_stroma", "Pericyte", "Mural" , "Smooth_muscle" )
filter_out_ct = unique(c(filter_out_ct, rm_ct))

# Pivot to matrix: rows = cell types, cols = DC1 bins
ct_mat_granular <- ct_frac_granular %>%
  dplyr::filter(!(cell_annotation_granular %in% filter_out_ct)) %>%
  select(DC1_bin30, cell_annotation_granular, frac) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = DC1_bin30, values_from = frac, values_fill = 0) %>%
  tibble::column_to_rownames("cell_annotation_granular") %>%
  as.matrix()

# Order columns by bin number
ct_mat_granular <- ct_mat_granular[, order(as.numeric(colnames(ct_mat_granular)))]

# Find position of bin 18 for vertical line
bin_cols_g <- as.numeric(colnames(ct_mat_granular))
bin18_idx_g <- which(bin_cols_g == 18)

# Z-score rows
ct_mat_granular_z <- zscore_rows(ct_mat_granular)

# Cluster rows
if (nrow(ct_mat_granular_z) > 1) {
  hc <- hclust(dist(ct_mat_granular_z), method = "ward.D2")
  row_order_g <- hc$order
} else {
  row_order_g <- 1
}

ht_granular <- Heatmap(
  ct_mat_granular_z[row_order_g, , drop = FALSE],
  col = col_fun,
  name = "Z-score (granular)",
  column_title = "Cell type composition shifts along DC1 (cell_annotation_granular)",
  row_title = "Cell type",
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_columns = FALSE,
  show_row_names = TRUE,
  row_names_side = "left",
  row_names_gp = gpar(fontsize = 8),
  show_column_names = FALSE,
  rect_gp = gpar(col = "lightgrey", lwd = 0.3),
  na_col = "grey90",
  column_title_gp = gpar(fontsize = 12, fontface = "bold")
)

draw(ht_granular)
decorate_heatmap_body("Z-score (granular)", {
  x_pos <- bin18_idx_g / ncol(ct_mat_granular_z)
  grid.lines(x = unit(x_pos, "npc"), y = unit(c(0, 1), "npc"), gp = gpar(col = "black", lwd = 2))
})

# Save
svg(file.path(FIG_DIR, "celltype_composition_dc1_granular.svg"), width = 12, height = 14)
draw(ht_granular)
decorate_heatmap_body("Z-score (granular)", {
  x_pos <- bin18_idx_g / ncol(ct_mat_granular_z)
  grid.lines(x = unit(x_pos, "npc"), y = unit(c(0, 1), "npc"), gp = gpar(col = "black", lwd = 2))
})
dev.off()
cat("Saved granular cell type heatmap\n")
```

```{r celltype_heatmap_granular_by_lineage, fig.width=10, fig.height=4, results='asis'}
# Separate heatmap per lineage with lineage-specific color scale

# Get lineage mapping from obs_df (cell_annotation_granular -> lineage)
ct_lineage_map <- obs_df %>%
  filter(!is.na(cell_annotation_granular), !is.na(lineage)) %>%
  distinct(cell_annotation_granular, lineage)

ct_lineage <- data.frame(
  cell_type = rownames(ct_mat_granular),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
) %>%
  left_join(ct_lineage_map, by = c("cell_type" = "cell_annotation_granular"))

ct_lineage$lineage[is.na(ct_lineage$lineage)] <- "Other"

# Order lineages
lineage_order <- c("Cancer", "Mac_Mono", "DC", "pDC", "Neutrophil",
                   "T_NK_ILC", "B_Plasma", "Fibroblast", "Stroma",
                   "Endothelial", "Epithelial", "Neural", "Other")

# Get lineages present in data
lineages_present <- intersect(lineage_order, unique(ct_lineage$lineage))

for (lin in lineages_present) {
  # Subset to this lineage
  ct_in_lin <- ct_lineage$cell_type[ct_lineage$lineage == lin]
  if (length(ct_in_lin) == 0) next

  mat_lin <- ct_mat_granular[ct_in_lin, , drop = FALSE]

  # Order rows alphabetically
  mat_lin <- mat_lin[order(rownames(mat_lin)), , drop = FALSE]

  # Lineage-specific color scale

  max_frac_lin <- max(mat_lin, na.rm = TRUE)
  if (max_frac_lin == 0) max_frac_lin <- 0.01
  col_fun_lin <- colorRamp2(c(0, max_frac_lin/2, max_frac_lin), c("white", "#fc8d59", "#b30000"))

  ht_lin <- Heatmap(
    mat_lin,
    col = col_fun_lin,
    name = "Fraction",
    column_title = paste0(lin, " — cell type fractions along DC1"),
    row_title = "Cell type",
    cluster_rows = FALSE,
    cluster_columns = FALSE,
    show_row_names = TRUE,
    row_names_side = "left",
    row_names_gp = gpar(fontsize = 9),
    show_column_names = FALSE,
    rect_gp = gpar(col = "lightgrey", lwd = 0.3),
    na_col = "grey90",
    column_title_gp = gpar(fontsize = 11, fontface = "bold"),
    heatmap_legend_param = list(title = "Fraction")
  )

  # Display
  draw(ht_lin)
  decorate_heatmap_body("Fraction", {
    x_pos <- bin18_idx_g / ncol(mat_lin)
    grid.lines(x = unit(x_pos, "npc"), y = unit(c(0, 1), "npc"), gp = gpar(col = "black", lwd = 2))
  })

  # Save
  n_rows <- nrow(mat_lin)
  fig_height <- max(3, n_rows * 0.4 + 1.5)
  svg(file.path(FIG_DIR, paste0("celltype_composition_dc1_", lin, ".svg")), width = 10, height = fig_height)
  draw(ht_lin)
  decorate_heatmap_body("Fraction", {
    x_pos <- bin18_idx_g / ncol(mat_lin)
    grid.lines(x = unit(x_pos, "npc"), y = unit(c(0, 1), "npc"), gp = gpar(col = "black", lwd = 2))
  })
  dev.off()

  cat("\n\n")  # spacing between plots in output
}
cat("Saved separate heatmaps per lineage\n")
```

## Cell type fraction line plots

Line plots showing cell type fractions along DC1, colored by `cell_annotation_granular`. Code related to plots in S7E. 

```{r celltype_lineplot_setup}
# Prepare data for line plots
ct_frac_granular_plot <- ct_frac_granular %>%
  left_join(ct_lineage_map, by = c("cell_annotation_granular" = "cell_annotation_granular")) %>%
  mutate(lineage = ifelse(is.na(lineage), "Other", lineage))

# Bin centers for x-axis
ct_frac_granular_plot <- ct_frac_granular_plot %>%
  mutate(DC1_bin_center = (DC1_bin30 - 0.5) / DC1_N_BINS)

# Lineages to plot (add/remove as needed)
LINEAGES_TO_PLOT <- c("Mac_Mono", "Fibroblast")
```

```{r celltype_lineplots, fig.width=10, fig.height=5}
MIN_NICHE_FRACTION = 0.025
for (lin in LINEAGES_TO_PLOT) {
  plot_data <- ct_frac_granular_plot %>%
    filter(lineage == lin)
  keep_ct = plot_data %>%
    dplyr::group_by(cell_annotation_granular) %>%
    summarise(max_frac = max(frac)) %>%
    dplyr::ungroup() %>%
    dplyr::filter(max_frac > MIN_NICHE_FRACTION) %>% .$cell_annotation_granular
  plot_data = plot_data %>%
    dplyr::filter(cell_annotation_granular %in% keep_ct)

  if (nrow(plot_data) == 0) {
    cat("No data for lineage:", lin, "\n")
    next
  }

  p <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = DC1_bin_center, y = frac, color = cell_annotation_granular)) +
    geom_smooth(linewidth = 1, se = T, method = 'loess') +
    #geom_point(size = 1.5) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 18 / DC1_N_BINS, linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 0.8) +
    labs(
      x = "Classical niche ablation response (DC1)",
      y = "Fraction of cells",
      title = paste0(lin, " — cell type fractions along DC1"),
      color = "Cell type"
    ) +
    theme_tufte() +
    rose_theme +
    theme(
      legend.position = "right",
      legend.text = element_text(size = 9),
      plot.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold")
    ) +
    scale_color_tableau()

  print(p)

  ggsave(file.path(FIG_DIR, paste0("celltype_fraction_lineplot_", lin, ".svg")),
         p, width = 10, height = 5)
}
cat("Saved line plots\n")
```

```{r celltype_lineplots_within_lineage, fig.width=10, fig.height=5}
# Fraction of each granular annotation within its lineage (e.g., among all fibroblasts)
MIN_NICHE_LINEAGE_FRACTION = 0.1

ct_frac_within_lineage <- obs_df %>%
  filter(!is.na(DC1_bin30), !is.na(cell_annotation_granular), !is.na(lineage)) %>%
  group_by(DC1_bin30, lineage, cell_annotation_granular) %>%
  summarize(n = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(DC1_bin30, lineage) %>%
  mutate(frac_within = n / sum(n)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(DC1_bin_center = (DC1_bin30 - 0.5) / DC1_N_BINS)

for (lin in LINEAGES_TO_PLOT) {
  plot_data <- ct_frac_within_lineage %>%
    filter(lineage == lin)
  
  keep_ct = plot_data %>%
    dplyr::group_by(cell_annotation_granular) %>%
    summarise(max_frac = max(frac_within)) %>%
    dplyr::ungroup() %>%
    dplyr::filter(max_frac > MIN_NICHE_LINEAGE_FRACTION) %>% .$cell_annotation_granular
  plot_data = plot_data %>%
    dplyr::filter(cell_annotation_granular %in% keep_ct)
  
  # remove non-TAM populations
  plot_data = plot_data %>% dplyr::filter(!(grepl("Macrophage", cell_annotation_granular)))

  if (nrow(plot_data) == 0) {
    cat("No data for lineage:", lin, "\n")
    next
  }

  p <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = DC1_bin_center, y = frac_within, color = cell_annotation_granular)) +
    geom_smooth(linewidth = 1, se = TRUE, method = 'loess') +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 18 / DC1_N_BINS, linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 0.8) +
    labs(
      x = "Classical niche ablation response (DC1)",
      y = paste0("Fraction within ", lin),
      title = paste0(lin, " — subtype fractions within lineage along DC1"),
      color = "Cell type"
    ) +
    theme_tufte() +
    rose_theme +
    theme(
      legend.position = "right",
      legend.text = element_text(size = 9),
      plot.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold")
    ) +
    scale_color_tableau()

  print(p)

  ggsave(file.path(FIG_DIR, paste0("celltype_fraction_within_lineage_lineplot_", lin, ".svg")),
         p, width = 10, height = 5)
}
cat("Saved within-lineage line plots\n")
```

---

# Linear Model Testing


## Tier1: Cell-type-specific ligand expression

```{r lm_ctmode}
# cell type mode: Sender-specific at cell_annotation level (more granular than Mode 1 lineage level)

# Build wide receptor fraction table for cell types
rec_ct_wide <- cov_bins
for (key in names(rec_celltype_bins)) {
  tmp <- rec_celltype_bins[[key]][, c("DC1_bin30", "rec_frac_bin")]
  colnames(tmp)[2] <- paste0("rec_ct_", key)
  rec_ct_wide <- left_join(rec_ct_wide, tmp, by = "DC1_bin30")
}

lm_rows_ctmode <- list()

for (lig_key in names(sender_celltype_bins)) {
  lig_df <- sender_celltype_bins[[lig_key]]
  parts  <- strsplit(lig_key, "__")[[1]]
  ct_s   <- parts[1]
  lig    <- parts[2]

  bin_wide <- inner_join(lig_df, rec_ct_wide, by = "DC1_bin30") %>%
    filter(!is.na(lig_expr_bin), n_sender >= 5)
  if (nrow(bin_wide) < 5) next

  lr_sub <- LR_PAIRS[LR_PAIRS$ligand == lig, ]

  rec_cols <- c()
  rec_meta <- list()
  for (j in seq_len(nrow(lr_sub))) {
    rec <- lr_sub$receptor_gene[j]
    for (ct_r in RECEIVER_CELLTYPES) {
      rc <- paste0("rec_ct_", rec, "__", ct_r)
      if (!rc %in% names(bin_wide)) next
      valid_rows <- !is.na(bin_wide[[rc]])
      if (sum(valid_rows) < 5) next
      rec_cols <- c(rec_cols, rc)
      rec_meta[[rc]] <- list(receptor = rec, receiver_celltype = ct_r,
                             pathway = lr_sub$pathway_name[j],
                             is_chemokine = lr_sub$is_chemokine[j],
                             is_chemokine_expanded = lr_sub$is_chemokine_expanded[j])
    }
  }
  if (length(rec_cols) == 0) next

  resp_mat <- as.matrix(bin_wide[, rec_cols, drop = FALSE])
  fit <- tryCatch(lm(resp_mat ~ lig_expr_bin, data = bin_wide), error = function(e) NULL)
  if (is.null(fit)) next

  fit_summ <- summary(fit)

  for (k in seq_along(rec_cols)) {
    rc <- rec_cols[k]
    meta <- rec_meta[[rc]]
    resp_summ <- if (length(rec_cols) == 1) fit_summ else fit_summ[[k]]
    if (is.null(resp_summ)) next
    s <- resp_summ$coefficients
    if (!"lig_expr_bin" %in% rownames(s)) next

    y_vals <- bin_wide[[rc]]
    valid_y <- !is.na(y_vals)
    pr <- if (sum(valid_y) >= 3) cor(bin_wide$lig_expr_bin[valid_y], y_vals[valid_y]) else NA_real_

    lm_rows_ctmode[[length(lm_rows_ctmode) + 1]] <- data.frame(
      mode              = "celltype",
      sender_celltype   = ct_s,
      sender_lineage    = as.character(LINEAGE_MAP[ct_s]),
      ligand            = lig,
      receiver_celltype = meta$receiver_celltype,
      receiver_lineage  = as.character(LINEAGE_MAP[meta$receiver_celltype]),
      receptor          = meta$receptor,
      pathway           = meta$pathway,
      is_chemokine      = meta$is_chemokine,
      is_chemokine_expanded = meta$is_chemokine_expanded,
      slope             = s["lig_expr_bin", "Estimate"],
      se                = s["lig_expr_bin", "Std. Error"],
      t_val             = s["lig_expr_bin", "t value"],
      pval              = s["lig_expr_bin", "Pr(>|t|)"],
      r2                = resp_summ$r.squared,
      pearson_r         = pr,
      n_bins            = sum(valid_y),
      stringsAsFactors  = FALSE
    )
  }
}

lm_df_ctmode <- if (length(lm_rows_ctmode) > 0) do.call(rbind, lm_rows_ctmode) else data.frame()
if (nrow(lm_df_ctmode) > 0) {
  lm_df_ctmode$padj <- p.adjust(lm_df_ctmode$pval, method = "BH")
  lm_df_ctmode      <- lm_df_ctmode[order(lm_df_ctmode$padj), ]
}
cat("cell type mode (cell type) pairs tested:", nrow(lm_df_ctmode), "\n")
cat("Significant (padj<0.1, slope>0):",
    sum(lm_df_ctmode$padj < 0.1 & lm_df_ctmode$slope > 0, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
print(head(lm_df_ctmode[lm_df_ctmode$slope > 0, ], 10))
```

## Tier1: Niche-summed ligand and receptor

```{r lm_nichemode}
# niche mode: Both ligand AND receptor are niche-summed (cancer cell perspective)
# This tests whether cancer cells in niches with high ligand also have high receptor+ neighbors

# Build wide niche receptor table
niche_rec_wide <- cov_bins
for (key in names(niche_rec_bins)) {
  tmp <- niche_rec_bins[[key]][, c("DC1_bin30", "niche_rec_bin")]
  colnames(tmp)[2] <- paste0("niche_rec_", key)
  niche_rec_wide <- left_join(niche_rec_wide, tmp, by = "DC1_bin30")
}

lm_rows_nichemode <- list()

# Loop over unique ligands
for (lig in names(niche_lig_bins)) {
  lig_df <- niche_lig_bins[[lig]]

  # Join with niche receptor wide table
  bin_wide <- inner_join(lig_df, niche_rec_wide[, !names(niche_rec_wide) %in%
                        # c("frac_basal_bin", "frac_mesen_bin", "n_cancer")],
                          c( "n_cancer")],
                         by = "DC1_bin30")

  # Filter valid covariate rows
  valid_cov <- !is.na(bin_wide$niche_lig_bin) #&
               #!is.na(bin_wide$frac_basal_bin) &
               #!is.na(bin_wide$frac_mesen_bin)
  bin_sub <- bin_wide[valid_cov, ]
  if (nrow(bin_sub) < 5) next

  # Add standardized ligand column
 bin_sub$lig_expr <- bin_sub$niche_lig_bin

  # Find all LR pairs for this ligand
  lr_sub <- LR_PAIRS[LR_PAIRS$ligand == lig, ]

  # Gather valid niche receptor columns (no lineage - summed across all cell types)
  rec_cols <- c()
  rec_meta <- list()
  for (j in seq_len(nrow(lr_sub))) {
    rec <- lr_sub$receptor_gene[j]
    rc <- paste0("niche_rec_", rec)
    if (!rc %in% names(bin_sub)) next
    valid_rows <- !is.na(bin_sub[[rc]])
    if (sum(valid_rows) < 5) next
    rec_cols <- c(rec_cols, rc)
    rec_meta[[rc]] <- list(receptor = rec,
                           pathway = lr_sub$pathway_name[j],
                           is_chemokine = lr_sub$is_chemokine[j],
                             is_chemokine_expanded = lr_sub$is_chemokine_expanded[j])
  }
  if (length(rec_cols) == 0) next

  # Fit multivariate lm
  resp_mat <- as.matrix(bin_sub[, rec_cols, drop = FALSE])
  fit <- tryCatch(
    #lm(resp_mat ~ lig_expr + frac_basal_bin + frac_mesen_bin, data = bin_sub),
    lm(resp_mat ~ lig_expr, data = bin_sub),
    error = function(e) NULL
  )
  if (is.null(fit)) next

  fit_summ <- summary(fit)

  for (k in seq_along(rec_cols)) {
    rc <- rec_cols[k]
    meta <- rec_meta[[rc]]

    resp_summ <- if (length(rec_cols) == 1) fit_summ else fit_summ[[k]]
    if (is.null(resp_summ)) next

    s <- resp_summ$coefficients
    if (!"lig_expr" %in% rownames(s)) next

    y_vals <- bin_sub[[rc]]
    valid_y <- !is.na(y_vals)
    pr <- if (sum(valid_y) >= 3) cor(bin_sub$lig_expr[valid_y], y_vals[valid_y]) else NA_real_

    lm_rows_nichemode[[length(lm_rows_nichemode) + 1]] <- data.frame(
      mode             = "niche_both",
      sender_lineage   = "all",
      ligand           = lig,
      receiver_lineage = "all",
      receptor         = meta$receptor,
      pathway          = meta$pathway,
      is_chemokine     = meta$is_chemokine,
      is_chemokine_expanded = meta$is_chemokine_expanded,
      slope            = s["lig_expr", "Estimate"],
      se               = s["lig_expr", "Std. Error"],
      t_val            = s["lig_expr", "t value"],
      pval             = s["lig_expr", "Pr(>|t|)"],
      r2               = resp_summ$r.squared,
      pearson_r        = pr,
      n_bins           = sum(valid_y),
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  }
}

lm_df_nichemode <- if (length(lm_rows_nichemode) > 0) do.call(rbind, lm_rows_nichemode) else data.frame()
if (nrow(lm_df_nichemode) > 0) {
  lm_df_nichemode$padj <- p.adjust(lm_df_nichemode$pval, method = "BH")
  lm_df_nichemode      <- lm_df_nichemode[order(lm_df_nichemode$padj), ]
}
cat("niche mode pairs tested:", nrow(lm_df_nichemode), "\n")
cat("Significant (padj<0.1, slope>0):",
    sum(lm_df_nichemode$padj < 0.1 & lm_df_nichemode$slope > 0, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
print(head(lm_df_nichemode[lm_df_nichemode$slope > 0, ], 10))
```

---

# Line Plots: Concordant Mode1/nichemode Chemokine Associations

For each significant nichemode chemokine receptor, plot trends for:
1. Receptor+ cell fraction for receiver lineages with significant Mode1 associations (same direction)
2. Ligand expression for sender lineages with significant Mode1 associations (faceted by ligand)

```{r filter_concordant_associations}
# Get significant nichemode chemokine receptor associations (padj < 0.05)
sig_nichemode_chemo <- lm_df_nichemode %>%
  filter(padj < 0.05, is_chemokine) %>%
  select(receptor, ligand, slope_nichemode = slope)

cat("Significant nichemode chemokine associations:", nrow(sig_nichemode_chemo), "\n")
print(sig_nichemode_chemo)

# Filter Mode1 for pval < 0.05 AND same direction as nichemode
concordant_mode1 <- lm_df_mode1 %>%
  filter(padj < 0.1, is_chemokine) %>%
  inner_join(sig_nichemode_chemo, by = c("receptor", "ligand")) %>%
  filter(sign(slope) == sign(slope_nichemode))

cat("\nConcordant Mode1 associations (pval < 0.05, same direction):", nrow(concordant_mode1), "\n")
print(concordant_mode1 %>% select(receptor, ligand, sender_lineage, receiver_lineage, slope, pval))
```

```{r build_bin_data_for_plots}
# Build bin-level data for receptor fraction and ligand expression
# Using existing rec_bins and sender_bins from earlier in notebook
# Note: rec_frac_bin = receptor+ cells of lineage / total niche cells (all lineages)

# Receptor fraction data: extract from rec_bins
rec_plot_df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(rec_bins), function(key) {
  parts <- strsplit(key, "__")[[1]]
  rec <- parts[1]
  lin <- parts[2]
  df <- rec_bins[[key]]
  df$receptor <- rec
  df$receiver_lineage <- lin
  df
}))

# Ligand expression data: extract from sender_bins (lig_expr_bin = mean log1p norm)
lig_plot_df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(sender_bins), function(key) {
  parts <- strsplit(key, "__")[[1]]
  lin <- parts[1]
  lig <- parts[2]
  df <- sender_bins[[key]]
  df$ligand <- lig
  df$sender_lineage <- lin
  df
}))

cat("Receptor bin data rows:", nrow(rec_plot_df), "\n")
cat("Ligand bin data rows:", nrow(lig_plot_df), "\n")
```

```{r plot_receptor_ligand_loess_function}
# Main plotting function using loess smoothing on bin-level data
# Stacks receptor plot on top, then one ligand plot per row below
plot_receptor_ligand_loess <- function(receptor, concordant_df, rec_plot_df, lig_plot_df,
                                       cov_bins) {

  # Get receiver lineages for this receptor
  receiver_lineages <- concordant_df %>%
    filter(receptor == !!receptor) %>%
    pull(receiver_lineage) %>%
    unique()

  # Get sender-ligand pairs for this receptor
  sender_df <- concordant_df %>%
    filter(receptor == !!receptor) %>%
    select(ligand, sender_lineage) %>%
    distinct()

  # Filter receptor data for this receptor and significant lineages
  rec_df <- rec_plot_df %>%
    filter(receptor == !!receptor, receiver_lineage %in% receiver_lineages)

  if (nrow(rec_df) == 0) {
    message("No receptor data for ", receptor)
    return(NULL)
  }

  # Scale receptor fraction per lineage (min-max to 0-1)
  rec_df <- rec_df %>%
    group_by(receiver_lineage) %>%
    mutate(rec_frac_scaled = (rec_frac_bin - min(rec_frac_bin, na.rm = TRUE)) /
                              (max(rec_frac_bin, na.rm = TRUE) - min(rec_frac_bin, na.rm = TRUE))) %>%
    ungroup()

  # Filter ligand data for significant sender-ligand pairs
  lig_df <- lig_plot_df %>%
    inner_join(sender_df, by = c("ligand", "sender_lineage"))

  ligands <- unique(lig_df$ligand)
  n_ligands <- length(ligands)
  has_ligands <- n_ligands > 0

  p_receptor <- ggplot(rec_df, aes(x = DC1_bin30, y = rec_frac_scaled,
                                   color = receiver_lineage, fill = receiver_lineage)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.6, size = 2) +
    geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = TRUE, alpha = 0.2, linewidth = 1) +
    labs(title = paste0(receptor, "+ cells in niche"),
         x = if (!has_ligands) "Classical niche ablation response (DC1 bin)" else NULL,
         y = "Scaled fraction (min-max)",
         color = "Receiver", fill = "Receiver") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
    theme(legend.position = "right",
          axis.text.x = if (has_ligands) element_blank() else element_text()) +
    rose_theme

  if (!has_ligands) {
    message("No ligand data for ", receptor)
    return(p_receptor)
  }

  # Create separate plot for each ligand
  ligand_plots <- lapply(seq_along(ligands), function(i) {
    lig <- ligands[i]
    lig_sub <- lig_df %>% filter(ligand == lig)
    is_last <- (i == n_ligands)

    p <- ggplot(lig_sub, aes(x = DC1_bin30, y = lig_expr_bin,
                             color = sender_lineage, fill = sender_lineage)) +
      geom_point(alpha = 0.6, size = 2) +
      geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = TRUE, alpha = 0.2, linewidth = 1) +
      labs(title = lig,
           x = if (is_last) "Classical niche ablation response (DC1 bin)" else NULL,
           y = "Mean log1p norm",
           color = "Sender", fill = "Sender") +
      theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
      theme(legend.position = "right",
            axis.text.x = if (is_last) element_text() else element_blank()) +
      rose_theme
    p
  })

  # Stack all plots vertically
  all_plots <- c(list(p_receptor), ligand_plots)
  wrap_plots(all_plots, ncol = 1)
}
```

### global level

Line plots showing global niche sums of ligand and receptor positive cell frequency along DC1 for specific chemokine receptors.

```{r global_lr_freq_lineplot_function}
plot_global_lr_freq_lines <- function(receptor_name, lr_pairs, niche_lig_bins, niche_rec_bins,
                                       dc1_n_bins, fig_dir, rm_ligands = NULL, rescale_data = FALSE) {
  # Get ligands for this receptor from LR_PAIRS
  # Use base R subsetting to avoid dplyr NSE issues with column name 'receptor'
  ligands <- lr_pairs$ligand[lr_pairs$receptor_gene == receptor_name]
  ligands <- unique(as.character(ligands))
  
  if(!(is.null(rm_ligands))){
    ligands = ligands[!(ligands %in% rm_ligands)]
  }

  # Filter to ligands that exist in niche_lig_bins
  ligands <- ligands[ligands %in% names(niche_lig_bins)]

  cat("Ligands for", receptor_name, ":", paste(ligands, collapse = ", "), "\n")

  if (length(ligands) == 0) {
    cat("No ligands found in niche_lig_bins for receptor:", receptor_name, "\n")
    return(NULL)
  }

  # Check receptor exists in niche_rec_bins
  if (!receptor_name %in% names(niche_rec_bins)) {
    cat("Receptor not found in niche_rec_bins:", receptor_name, "\n")
    return(NULL)
  }

  # Build combined data frame
  plot_data_list <- list()

  # Add ligand data
  for (lig in ligands) {
    lig_df <- niche_lig_bins[[lig]]
    if (is.null(lig_df) || nrow(lig_df) == 0) next
    plot_data_list[[lig]] <- data.frame(
      DC1_bin30 = lig_df$DC1_bin30,
      DC1_bin_center = (lig_df$DC1_bin30 - 0.5) / dc1_n_bins,
      freq = lig_df$niche_lig_bin,
      gene = lig,
      type = "ligand",
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  }

  # Add receptor data
  rec_df <- niche_rec_bins[[receptor_name]]
  if (!is.null(rec_df) && nrow(rec_df) > 0) {
    plot_data_list[[receptor_name]] <- data.frame(
      DC1_bin30 = rec_df$DC1_bin30,
      DC1_bin_center = (rec_df$DC1_bin30 - 0.5) / dc1_n_bins,
      freq = rec_df$niche_rec_bin,
      gene = receptor_name,
      type = "receptor",
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  }

  plot_data <- do.call(rbind, plot_data_list)
  
  # rescale if necessary
  if(rescale_data){
    plot_data = plot_data %>%
      dplyr::group_by(gene) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(freq = scales::rescale(freq)) %>%
      ungroup()
  }

  if (nrow(plot_data) == 0) {
    cat("No data to plot for receptor:", receptor_name, "\n")
    return(NULL)
  }

  # Create color palette - distinct colors for each gene
  all_genes <- unique(plot_data$gene)
  n_colors <- length(all_genes)
  if (n_colors <= 8) {
    color_pal <- RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(max(3, n_colors), "Set2")[1:n_colors]
  } else {
    color_pal <- colorRampPalette(RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(8, "Set2"))(n_colors)
  }
  names(color_pal) <- all_genes

  # Plot
  p <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = DC1_bin_center, y = freq, color = gene,fill = gene, linetype = type)) +
    geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = T, linewidth = 1.2, span = 0.3, alpha = .2) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 18 / dc1_n_bins, linetype = "dotted", color = "grey40", linewidth = 0.8) +
    scale_linetype_manual(values = c("ligand" = "solid", "receptor" = "dashed")) +
    scale_color_manual(values = color_pal) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = color_pal) +
    labs(
      x = X_LAB,
      y = "Scaled amount",
      title = paste0(receptor_name, " and its ligands along DC1"),
      color = "Gene",
      linetype = "Type"
    ) +
    theme_tufte() +
    rose_theme +
    theme(
      legend.position = "right",
      plot.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold")
    )

  #print(p)

  # Save
  save_path <- file.path(fig_dir, paste0("global_lr_freq_", receptor_name, ".svg"))
  ggsave(save_path, p, width = 8, height = 5)
  cat("Saved:", save_path, "\n")

  return(p)
}
```

```{r global_lr_freq_lineplots, fig.width=8, fig.height=5}
# Plot Cxcr3 and its ligands (Cxcl9, Cxcl10)
plot_global_lr_freq_lines("Cxcr3", LR_PAIRS, niche_lig_bins, niche_rec_bins, DC1_N_BINS, FIG_DIR, rm_ligands = c('Pf4', 'Cxcl13'), rescale_data = T)

# Plot Cxcr6 and its ligands (Cxcl16)
plot_global_lr_freq_lines("Cxcr6", LR_PAIRS, niche_lig_bins, niche_rec_bins, DC1_N_BINS, FIG_DIR, rescale_data = T)

# Plot Cxcr6 and its ligands (Cxcl16)
plot_global_lr_freq_lines("Cxcr4", LR_PAIRS, niche_lig_bins, niche_rec_bins, DC1_N_BINS, FIG_DIR, rescale_data = T)

```


# Cytokine Ligand–Response Program Analysis

Test associations between cytokine ligand expression and response gene program activation across the DC1 trajectory.

# Ligand-Program Response Association Analysis

Test individual ligand expression (from sender_bins) against program response scores across DC1 bins.

## Compute response gene expression bins

For response genes in cytokine_signatures not already in sender_bins, compute pseudobulk expression per lineage per bin.

```{r compute_response_gene_bins}
all_response_genes <- unique(unlist(cytokine_signatures))
cat("Total response genes across programs:", length(all_response_genes), "\n")

response_gene_bins <- list()
genes_computed <- 0

for (lin in SENDER_LINEAGES) {
  sub <- obs_df[!is.na(obs_df$lineage) & obs_df$lineage == lin & !is.na(obs_df$DC1_bin30), ]
  if (nrow(sub) < 10) next

  for (gene in all_response_genes) {
    existing_key <- paste(lin, gene, sep = "__")

    # Skip if already in sender_bins
    if (existing_key %in% names(sender_bins)) {
      response_gene_bins[[existing_key]] <- sender_bins[[existing_key]]
      next
    }

    # Check if gene exists in data
    if (!gene %in% names(sub)) next

    # Apply same filtering as ligands (OR logic: pct >= MIN_PCT OR n_cells >= MIN_LIG_CELLS)
    gene_pct <- mean(sub[[gene]] > 0, na.rm = TRUE)
    gene_n_cells <- sum(sub[[gene]] > 0, na.rm = TRUE)
    if (gene_pct < MIN_PCT && gene_n_cells < MIN_LIG_CELLS) next

    tmp <- sub %>%
      group_by(DC1_bin30) %>%
      summarize(
        lig_expr_bin = log1p(sum(.data[[gene]], na.rm = TRUE) /
                             pmax(sum(lib_size, na.rm = TRUE), 1)),
        n_sender = n(),
        n_lig_cells = sum(.data[[gene]] > 0, na.rm = TRUE),
        .groups = "drop"
      )

    response_gene_bins[[existing_key]] <- tmp
    genes_computed <- genes_computed + 1
  }
}

cat("Response gene bins computed:", genes_computed, "new +",
    length(response_gene_bins) - genes_computed, "from sender_bins\n")
```

## Compute program response scores per lineage per bin

Mean Z-scaled expression of response genes within each program.

```{r compute_program_response_scores}
program_response_scores <- list()
bin_ids <- sort(unique(cov_bins$DC1_bin30))

for (prog in names(cytokine_signatures)) {
  response_genes <- cytokine_signatures[[prog]]

  for (lin in SENDER_LINEAGES) {
    # Find which response genes have bin data for this lineage
    available_genes <- character(0)
    for (gene in response_genes) {
      key <- paste(lin, gene, sep = "__")
      if (key %in% names(response_gene_bins)) {
        available_genes <- c(available_genes, gene)
      }
    }

    if (length(available_genes) < 1) next  # Need at least 1 gene

    # Build matrix: rows = genes, cols = bins
    gene_mat <- matrix(NA, nrow = length(available_genes), ncol = length(bin_ids))
    rownames(gene_mat) <- available_genes

    for (i in seq_along(available_genes)) {
      gene <- available_genes[i]
      key <- paste(lin, gene, sep = "__")
      gene_df <- response_gene_bins[[key]]
      for (j in seq_along(bin_ids)) {
        idx <- which(gene_df$DC1_bin30 == bin_ids[j])
        if (length(idx) > 0) gene_mat[i, j] <- gene_df$lig_expr_bin[idx]
      }
    }

    # Z-scale each gene across bins (within lineage)
    z_mat <- t(apply(gene_mat, 1, function(x) {
      if (sd(x, na.rm = TRUE) == 0) return(rep(0, length(x)))
      (x - mean(x, na.rm = TRUE)) / sd(x, na.rm = TRUE)
    }))

    # Average Z-scores to get program response score per bin
    response_score <- colMeans(z_mat, na.rm = TRUE)

    result_key <- paste(lin, prog, sep = "__")
    program_response_scores[[result_key]] <- data.frame(
      DC1_bin30 = bin_ids,
      response_score = response_score,
      n_genes = length(available_genes)
    )
  }
}

cat("Program response scores computed:", length(program_response_scores), "lineage-program pairs\n")
```

## Linear model: ligand expression → program response score

Test all panel_cyto_genes ligands against all signal_anno_broad programs across sender × receiver lineage combinations.

```{r ligand_program_lm}
lm_rows_lig_prog <- list()

for (lig in panel_cyto_genes) {
  for (sender_lin in SENDER_LINEAGES) {
    lig_key <- paste(sender_lin, lig, sep = "__")
    if (!lig_key %in% names(sender_bins)) next

    lig_df <- sender_bins[[lig_key]]

    # Test against all programs × receiver lineages
    for (prog in names(cytokine_signatures)) {
      for (receiver_lin in RECEIVER_LINEAGES) {
        prog_key <- paste(receiver_lin, prog, sep = "__")
        if (!prog_key %in% names(program_response_scores)) next

        prog_df <- program_response_scores[[prog_key]]

        # Merge ligand expression with program response score
        merged <- inner_join(lig_df, prog_df, by = "DC1_bin30")
        merged <- merged[!is.na(merged$lig_expr_bin) & !is.na(merged$response_score) &
                         merged$n_sender >= 5, ]
        if (nrow(merged) < 5) next

        # Z-scale lig_expr_bin across bins
        if (sd(merged$lig_expr_bin, na.rm = TRUE) > 0) {
          merged$lig_expr_z <- (merged$lig_expr_bin - mean(merged$lig_expr_bin, na.rm = TRUE)) /
                                sd(merged$lig_expr_bin, na.rm = TRUE)
        } else {
          next
        }

        # Fit lm: response_score ~ lig_expr_z (both Z-scaled)
        fit <- tryCatch(
          lm(response_score ~ lig_expr_z, data = merged),
          error = function(e) NULL
        )
        if (is.null(fit)) next

        s <- summary(fit)$coefficients
        if (!"lig_expr_z" %in% rownames(s)) next

        pr <- cor(merged$response_score, merged$lig_expr_z, use = "complete.obs")

        lm_rows_lig_prog[[length(lm_rows_lig_prog) + 1]] <- data.frame(
          ligand = lig,
          sender_lineage = sender_lin,
          receiver_lineage = receiver_lin,
          program = prog,
          slope = s["lig_expr_z", "Estimate"],
          se = s["lig_expr_z", "Std. Error"],
          t_val = s["lig_expr_z", "t value"],
          pval = s["lig_expr_z", "Pr(>|t|)"],
          r2 = summary(fit)$r.squared,
          pearson_r = pr,
          n_bins = nrow(merged),
          n_response_genes = prog_df$n_genes[1],
          stringsAsFactors = FALSE
        )
      }
    }
  }
}

lm_df_lig_prog <- if (length(lm_rows_lig_prog) > 0) do.call(rbind, lm_rows_lig_prog) else data.frame()
if (nrow(lm_df_lig_prog) > 0) {
  lm_df_lig_prog$padj <- p.adjust(lm_df_lig_prog$pval, method = "BH")
  lm_df_lig_prog <- lm_df_lig_prog[order(lm_df_lig_prog$padj), ]
}

cat("Ligand-program pairs tested:", nrow(lm_df_lig_prog), "\n")
cat("Significant (padj<0.05, slope>0):", sum(lm_df_lig_prog$padj < 0.05 & lm_df_lig_prog$slope > 0, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
cat("Significant (padj<0.05, slope<0):", sum(lm_df_lig_prog$padj < 0.05 & lm_df_lig_prog$slope < 0, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
print(head(lm_df_lig_prog[lm_df_lig_prog$slope > 0, ], 15))

write.csv(lm_df_lig_prog, file.path(FIG_DIR, "ligand_program_lm_results.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
```

```{r}
# read in if necessary
lm_df_lig_prog <- readr::read_csv(file.path(FIG_DIR, "ligand_program_lm_results.csv"))
```

```{r}
# save computed program scores
saveRDS(program_response_scores, file = file.path(PRECOMPUTED_DIR, 'program_response_scores_bin.rds'))
```


## response program correlation

Compute Spearman correlation between program response scores and DC1 bin number to identify programs that increase or decrease along the trajectory.

```{r program_dc1_correlation}
program_dc1_cor <- list()

for (key in names(program_response_scores)) {
  prog_df <- program_response_scores[[key]]

  # Skip if too few bins
  if (nrow(prog_df) < 5) next

  # Spearman correlation: response_score ~ DC1_bin30
  cor_test <- cor.test(prog_df$DC1_bin30, prog_df$response_score, method = "spearman", exact = FALSE)

  # Parse lineage and program from key
  parts <- strsplit(key, "__")[[1]]
  lineage <- parts[1]
  program <- parts[2]

  program_dc1_cor[[length(program_dc1_cor) + 1]] <- data.frame(
    lineage = lineage,
    program = program,
    spearman_rho = cor_test$estimate,
    pval = cor_test$p.value,
    n_bins = nrow(prog_df),
    n_genes = prog_df$n_genes[1]
  )
}

program_dc1_cor_df <- do.call(rbind, program_dc1_cor)
rownames(program_dc1_cor_df) <- NULL

# FDR correction
program_dc1_cor_df$padj <- p.adjust(program_dc1_cor_df$pval, method = "BH")

# Sort by rho
program_dc1_cor_df <- program_dc1_cor_df %>% arrange(desc(spearman_rho))

cat("Program-DC1 correlations computed:", nrow(program_dc1_cor_df), "\n")
cat("  Significant (padj < 0.05):", sum(program_dc1_cor_df$padj < 0.05), "\n")
cat("  Positive (rho > 0):", sum(program_dc1_cor_df$spearman_rho > 0 & program_dc1_cor_df$padj < 0.05), "\n")
cat("  Negative (rho < 0):", sum(program_dc1_cor_df$spearman_rho < 0 & program_dc1_cor_df$padj < 0.05), "\n")

# Save
write.csv(program_dc1_cor_df, file.path(FIG_DIR, "program_dc1_spearman_correlations.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
cat("Saved to:", file.path(FIG_DIR, "program_dc1_spearman_correlations.csv"), "\n")
```

```{r program_dc1_cor_table}
# Show top positively and negatively correlated programs
cat("\nTop 10 positively correlated programs:\n")
print(head(program_dc1_cor_df %>% filter(spearman_rho > 0), 10))

cat("\nTop 10 negatively correlated programs:\n")
print(head(program_dc1_cor_df %>% filter(spearman_rho < 0) %>% arrange(spearman_rho), 10))
```

## Heatmap visualization: ligand → receptor → program response

```{r plot_program_heatmap_lineage_fn}
plot_program_heatmap_lineage <- function(sig_df, sender_bins, rec_bins, program_response_scores,
                                          cov_bins, LR_PAIRS, DC1_N_BINS,
                                          title_suffix, file_suffix, FIG_DIR,
                                          normalize = "zscore", program_order = NULL,
                                          include_receptors = FALSE) {
  if (nrow(sig_df) == 0) {
    cat("No pairs for", title_suffix, "— skipping.\n")
    return(NULL)
  }

  bin_ids <- sort(unique(cov_bins$DC1_bin30))
  n_bins <- length(bin_ids)
  bin_centres <- (bin_ids - 0.5) / DC1_N_BINS

  programs <- if (!is.null(program_order)) {
    intersect(program_order, unique(sig_df$program))
  } else {
    unique(sig_df$program)
  }

  # Build row_info: gene_type = "ligand", "receptor", or "program"
  row_info <- data.frame(
    gene = character(),
    lineage = character(),
    program_group = character(),
    gene_type = character(),
    bin_key = character(),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )

  for (prog in programs) {
    prog_df <- sig_df[sig_df$program == prog, ]

    # Get unique ligand-sender-receiver combinations for this program
    lig_lin <- unique(prog_df[, c("ligand", "sender_lineage", "receiver_lineage")])

    for (i in seq_len(nrow(lig_lin))) {
      lig <- lig_lin$ligand[i]
      sender_lin <- lig_lin$sender_lineage[i]
      receiver_lin <- lig_lin$receiver_lineage[i]

      # Add ligand row (sender lineage)
      bin_key <- paste(sender_lin, lig, sep = "__")
      if (bin_key %in% names(sender_bins)) {
        row_info <- rbind(row_info, data.frame(
          gene = lig, lineage = sender_lin, program_group = prog,
          gene_type = "ligand", bin_key = bin_key, stringsAsFactors = FALSE
        ))
      }

      # Add receptor rows if requested (receiver lineage)
      if (include_receptors) {
        # Find receptors for this ligand from LR_PAIRS
        lig_receptors <- unique(LR_PAIRS$receptor_gene[LR_PAIRS$ligand == lig])
        for (rec in lig_receptors) {
          rec_key <- paste(rec, receiver_lin, sep = "__")
          if (rec_key %in% names(rec_bins)) {
            row_info <- rbind(row_info, data.frame(
              gene = rec, lineage = receiver_lin, program_group = prog,
              gene_type = "receptor", bin_key = rec_key, stringsAsFactors = FALSE
            ))
          }
        }
      }
    }

    # Add program response score row (receiver lineages)
    prog_receiver_lineages <- unique(prog_df$receiver_lineage)
    for (lin in prog_receiver_lineages) {
      prog_key <- paste(lin, prog, sep = "__")
      if (prog_key %in% names(program_response_scores)) {
        row_info <- rbind(row_info, data.frame(
          gene = prog, lineage = lin, program_group = prog,
          gene_type = "program", bin_key = prog_key, stringsAsFactors = FALSE
        ))
      }
    }
  }

  # Remove duplicates
  row_info <- row_info[!duplicated(paste(row_info$gene, row_info$lineage, row_info$program_group, row_info$gene_type)), ]

  if (nrow(row_info) == 0) {
    cat("No valid rows for", title_suffix, "— skipping.\n")
    return(NULL)
  }

  # Build matrix
  mat <- matrix(NA, nrow = nrow(row_info), ncol = n_bins)
  row_labels <- paste0(row_info$gene, " (", row_info$lineage, ")")
  rownames(mat) <- row_labels
  colnames(mat) <- sprintf("%.2f", bin_centres)

  for (i in seq_len(nrow(row_info))) {
    gtype <- row_info$gene_type[i]
    bin_key <- row_info$bin_key[i]

    if (gtype == "ligand" && bin_key %in% names(sender_bins)) {
      bin_df <- sender_bins[[bin_key]]
      val_col <- "lig_expr_bin"
    } else if (gtype == "receptor" && bin_key %in% names(rec_bins)) {
      bin_df <- rec_bins[[bin_key]]
      val_col <- "rec_frac_bin"
    } else if (gtype == "program" && bin_key %in% names(program_response_scores)) {
      bin_df <- program_response_scores[[bin_key]]
      val_col <- "response_score"
    } else {
      next
    }

    if (!is.null(bin_df) && val_col %in% names(bin_df)) {
      for (j in seq_along(bin_ids)) {
        idx <- which(bin_df$DC1_bin30 == bin_ids[j])
        if (length(idx) > 0) mat[i, j] <- bin_df[[val_col]][idx]
      }
    }
  }

  # Smooth rows: 3-bin rolling average
  smooth_rows <- function(m) {
    n_cols <- ncol(m)
    smoothed <- m
    for (i in seq_len(nrow(m))) {
      for (j in seq_len(n_cols)) {
        neighbors <- max(1, j-1):min(n_cols, j+1)
        smoothed[i, j] <- mean(m[i, neighbors], na.rm = TRUE)
      }
    }
    smoothed
  }
  mat <- smooth_rows(mat)

  # Normalize rows
  if (normalize == "minmax") {
    norm_mat <- t(apply(mat, 1, function(x) {
      rng <- range(x, na.rm = TRUE)
      if (rng[2] == rng[1]) return(rep(0.5, length(x)))
      (x - rng[1]) / (rng[2] - rng[1])
    }))
    legend_name <- "Min-max"
    col_fun <- colorRamp2(c(0, 0.5, 1), c("#f7f7f7", "#fc8d59", "#b30000"))
  } else {
    norm_mat <- t(apply(mat, 1, function(x) {
      if (sd(x, na.rm = TRUE) == 0) return(rep(0, length(x)))
      (x - mean(x, na.rm = TRUE)) / sd(x, na.rm = TRUE)
    }))
    legend_name <- "Z-score"
    col_fun <- colorRamp2(c(-2, 0, 2), c("#4575b4", "white", "#d73027"))
  }
  colnames(norm_mat) <- colnames(mat)

  # Order rows: ligands first, then receptors, then programs; within each by onset
  get_onset_bin <- function(x) {
    if (all(is.na(x)) || length(x) < 2) return(Inf)
    deriv <- diff(x)
    onset <- which(deriv > 0.05 * max(abs(deriv), na.rm = TRUE))[1]
    if (is.na(onset)) return(Inf)
    onset
  }
  row_info$onset_bin <- apply(norm_mat, 1, get_onset_bin)

  # Order: ligands first, receptors second, programs last; then by onset
  row_info$gene_type_order <- match(row_info$gene_type, c("ligand", "receptor", "program"))
  row_info <- row_info %>%
    group_by(program_group) %>%
    arrange(gene_type_order, onset_bin) %>%
    ungroup()

  new_row_labels <- paste0(row_info$gene, " (", row_info$lineage, ")")
  norm_mat <- norm_mat[new_row_labels, , drop = FALSE]

  # Row annotation
  row_ha <- rowAnnotation(
    type = row_info$gene_type,
    col = list(type = c("ligand" = "forestgreen", "receptor" = "#fc8d59", "program" = "#8856a7")),
    show_legend = TRUE,
    annotation_name_side = "top"
  )

  # Program group levels
  prog_levels <- if (!is.null(program_order)) {
    intersect(program_order, unique(row_info$program_group))
  } else {
    unique(row_info$program_group)
  }

  # Create heatmap
  ht <- Heatmap(
    norm_mat,
    col = col_fun,
    name = legend_name,
    row_split = factor(row_info$program_group, levels = prog_levels),
    row_title_rot = 0,
    row_title_gp = gpar(fontsize = 10, fontface = "bold"),
    cluster_rows = FALSE,
    cluster_columns = FALSE,
    show_row_names = TRUE,
    row_names_side = "left",
    row_names_gp = gpar(fontsize = 8),
    show_column_names = FALSE,
    column_title = "Classical response axis",
    column_title_side = "bottom",
    rect_gp = gpar(col = "lightgrey", lwd = 0.3),
    na_col = "grey90",
    left_annotation = row_ha,
    heatmap_legend_param = list(title = legend_name, direction = "vertical")
  )

  draw(ht, column_title = paste("Ligand-Program Associations:", title_suffix))

  # Save
  n_rows <- nrow(norm_mat)
  pdf_path <- file.path(FIG_DIR, paste0("ligand_program_heatmap_", file_suffix, ".pdf"))
  pdf(pdf_path, width = 8, height = max(4, n_rows * 0.25 + 2))
  draw(ht, column_title = paste("Ligand-Program Associations:", title_suffix))
  dev.off()
```
