```{r plot_program_heatmap_lineage_fn}
  cat("Ligand-program heatmap saved to:", pdf_path, "\n")
  return(ht)
}
```

## Plot significant ligand-program associations

```{r plot_ligand_program_heatmaps, fig.width=6, fig.height=20}
sig_lig_prog.pos <- lm_df_lig_prog %>%
  filter(padj < 0.01 & slope > 0.8) %>%
  arrange(program, desc(pearson_r))

if (nrow(sig_lig_prog.pos) > 0) {
  plot_program_heatmap_lineage(
    sig_lig_prog.pos, sender_bins, rec_bins, program_response_scores,
    cov_bins, LR_PAIRS, DC1_N_BINS,
    "significant (padj<0.01, positive)", "positive", FIG_DIR,
    include_receptors = FALSE
  )
} else {
  cat("No significant positive ligand-program associations to plot.\n")
}
```

```{r plot_ligand_program_heatmaps, fig.width=6, fig.height=20}
sig_lig_prog.neg <- lm_df_lig_prog %>%
  filter(padj < 0.0001 & slope < -0.8) %>%
  arrange(program, desc(pearson_r))

if (nrow(sig_lig_prog.neg) > 0) {
  plot_program_heatmap_lineage(
    sig_lig_prog.neg, sender_bins, rec_bins, program_response_scores,
    cov_bins, LR_PAIRS, DC1_N_BINS,
    "significant (padj<0.01, negative)", "negative", FIG_DIR,
    include_receptors = FALSE
  )
} else {
  cat("No significant positive ligand-program associations to plot.\n")
}
```

---



## DC1 correlation

Compute Spearman correlation between gene expression/frequency (per DC1 bin) and DC1 bin number for each cell type. This identifies genes (ligands and receptors) whose expression most strongly tracks the ablation response axis.

```{r gene_dc1_spearman_correlation}
# Thresholds for filtering significant correlations
COR_PADJ_THRESH <- 0.01
COR_RHO_THRESH  <- 0.7

# Compute Spearman correlation for each cell type × gene pair
gene_dc1_cor_results <- list()

# Process ligands from sender_celltype_bins
for (key in names(sender_celltype_bins)) {
  parts <- strsplit(key, "__")[[1]]
  ct <- parts[1]
  gene <- parts[2]

  bin_df <- sender_celltype_bins[[key]]

  # Need at least 5 bins for meaningful correlation
  if (nrow(bin_df) < 5) next

  # Spearman correlation between expression and DC1 bin
  cor_test <- tryCatch(
    cor.test(bin_df$DC1_bin30, bin_df$lig_expr_bin, method = "spearman", exact = FALSE),
    error = function(e) NULL
  )

  if (is.null(cor_test)) next

  gene_dc1_cor_results[[length(gene_dc1_cor_results) + 1]] <- data.frame(
    cell_type = ct,
    gene = gene,
    gene_type = "ligand",
    spearman_rho = cor_test$estimate,
    pval = cor_test$p.value,
    n_bins = nrow(bin_df),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}

# Process receptors from rec_celltype_bins
for (key in names(rec_celltype_bins)) {
  parts <- strsplit(key, "__")[[1]]
  gene <- parts[1]  # receptor name is first in rec_celltype_bins keys

  ct <- parts[2]    # cell type is second

  bin_df <- rec_celltype_bins[[key]]

  # Need at least 5 bins for meaningful correlation
  if (nrow(bin_df) < 5) next

  # Spearman correlation between receptor fraction and DC1 bin
  cor_test <- tryCatch(
    cor.test(bin_df$DC1_bin30, bin_df$rec_frac_bin, method = "spearman", exact = FALSE),
    error = function(e) NULL
  )

  if (is.null(cor_test)) next

  gene_dc1_cor_results[[length(gene_dc1_cor_results) + 1]] <- data.frame(
    cell_type = ct,
    gene = gene,
    gene_type = "receptor",
    spearman_rho = cor_test$estimate,
    pval = cor_test$p.value,
    n_bins = nrow(bin_df),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}

gene_dc1_cor_df <- do.call(rbind, gene_dc1_cor_results)
rownames(gene_dc1_cor_df) <- NULL

# Adjust p-values
gene_dc1_cor_df$padj <- p.adjust(gene_dc1_cor_df$pval, method = "BH")

# Add lineage annotation
gene_dc1_cor_df$lineage <- LINEAGE_MAP[gene_dc1_cor_df$cell_type]

# Add chemokine annotation
gene_dc1_cor_df$is_chemokine <- gene_dc1_cor_df$gene %in% c(CHEMOKINE_LIGANDS, CHEMOKINE_RECEPTORS)

# Sort by absolute correlation
gene_dc1_cor_df <- gene_dc1_cor_df %>%
  arrange(desc(abs(spearman_rho)))

# Filter significant correlations
gene_dc1_cor_sig <- gene_dc1_cor_df %>%
  filter(padj < COR_PADJ_THRESH, abs(spearman_rho) > COR_RHO_THRESH)

cat("Gene-DC1 correlations computed:", nrow(gene_dc1_cor_df), "\n")
cat("  Ligands:", sum(gene_dc1_cor_df$gene_type == "ligand"), "\n")
cat("  Receptors:", sum(gene_dc1_cor_df$gene_type == "receptor"), "\n")
cat("Thresholds: padj <", COR_PADJ_THRESH, ", |rho| >", COR_RHO_THRESH, "\n")
cat("Significant:", nrow(gene_dc1_cor_sig), "\n")
cat("  Ligands:", sum(gene_dc1_cor_sig$gene_type == "ligand"), "\n")
cat("  Receptors:", sum(gene_dc1_cor_sig$gene_type == "receptor"), "\n")
cat("  Positive (rho >", COR_RHO_THRESH, "):", sum(gene_dc1_cor_sig$spearman_rho > 0, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
cat("  Negative (rho <", -COR_RHO_THRESH, "):", sum(gene_dc1_cor_sig$spearman_rho < 0, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")

# Show top associations
print(head(gene_dc1_cor_sig, 20))
```

```{r gene_dc1_cor_save}
# Save results for downstream analysis
write.csv(gene_dc1_cor_df,
          file.path(FIG_DIR, "gene_dc1_spearman_correlations.csv"),
          row.names = FALSE)
cat("Saved gene-DC1 correlations to:", file.path(FIG_DIR, "gene_dc1_spearman_correlations.csv"), "\n")
```

```{r}
# read in if necessary
gene_dc1_cor_df = readr::read_csv(file.path(FIG_DIR, "gene_dc1_spearman_correlations.csv"))
# join to ligand expression information
gene_dc1_cor_df = left_join(gene_dc1_cor_df, ligand_metrics, by = c('cell_type', 'gene', 'lineage'))
# join to expressino metrics
gene_dc1_cor_sig <- gene_dc1_cor_df %>%
  dplyr::filter(padj < COR_PADJ_THRESH, abs(spearman_rho) > COR_RHO_THRESH) %>%
  left_join(ligand_metrics, by = c('cell_type', 'gene'))
```


```{r gene_dc1_cor_heatmap_setup}
# Build expression matrix for significant gene-cell type pairs
# Rows: gene (cell_type), Columns: DC1 bins
# Handles both ligands (from sender_celltype_bins) and receptors (from rec_celltype_bins)

build_gene_expr_matrix <- function(sig_df, sender_bins, rec_bins, bin_ids) {
  if (nrow(sig_df) == 0) return(NULL)

  # Create row labels
sig_df$row_label <- paste0(sig_df$gene, " (", sig_df$cell_type, ")")

  # Build matrix
  mat <- matrix(NA, nrow = nrow(sig_df), ncol = length(bin_ids))
  rownames(mat) <- sig_df$row_label
  colnames(mat) <- bin_ids

  for (i in seq_len(nrow(sig_df))) {
    gene_type <- sig_df$gene_type[i]
    gene <- sig_df$gene[i]
    ct <- sig_df$cell_type[i]

    if (gene_type == "ligand") {
      key <- paste(ct, gene, sep = "__")
      if (!key %in% names(sender_bins)) next
      bin_df <- sender_bins[[key]]
      val_col <- "lig_expr_bin"
    } else {
      key <- paste(gene, ct, sep = "__")
      if (!key %in% names(rec_bins)) next
      bin_df <- rec_bins[[key]]
      val_col <- "rec_frac_bin"
    }

    for (j in seq_along(bin_ids)) {
      idx <- which(bin_df$DC1_bin30 == bin_ids[j])
      if (length(idx) > 0) mat[i, j] <- bin_df[[val_col]][idx]
    }
  }

  return(list(mat = mat, sig_df = sig_df))
}

# Z-score rows
zscore_rows <- function(mat) {
  t(apply(mat, 1, function(x) {
    if (sd(x, na.rm = TRUE) == 0) return(rep(0, length(x)))
    (x - mean(x, na.rm = TRUE)) / sd(x, na.rm = TRUE)
  }))
}

# Smooth rows (3-bin rolling average)
smooth_rows <- function(mat) {
  n_cols <- ncol(mat)
  smoothed <- mat
  for (i in seq_len(nrow(mat))) {
    for (j in seq_len(n_cols)) {
      neighbors <- max(1, j-1):min(n_cols, j+1)
      smoothed[i, j] <- mean(mat[i, neighbors], na.rm = TRUE)
    }
  }
  smoothed
}

# Get bin IDs from cov_bins
bin_ids <- sort(unique(cov_bins$DC1_bin30))
bin18_idx <- which(bin_ids == 18)
```


### ligand correlation heatmaps

```{r gene_dc1_cor_heatmap_positive, fig.width=8, fig.height=10}
# Filter to only cell types I want to use
celltype_rm <- c(
  "Neuron", "Granulocyte", "Adipocyte", "ILC3", "Schwann_cell",
  "Innate-like_T", "ILC2", "MAIT", "Mesenchymal-like_stroma",
  "Pericyte", "Mural", "Smooth_muscle", "Ductal", "Tuft-like",
  "Islet", "ADM", "Acinar",
  "Endothelial_lymphatic", "PSC", "Lymphoid_proliferating",
  "Mesothelial/apCAF"
)

# Positive correlations heatmap
sig_pos <- gene_dc1_cor_sig %>%
  dplyr::filter(spearman_rho > 0) %>%
  dplyr::filter(gene_type == 'ligand' & !(is_chemokine)) %>%
  dplyr::filter(pct_expressing > 0.05 | gene == 'Ifn_I') %>%
  dplyr::filter(!(cell_type %in% celltype_rm)) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(gene, desc(spearman_rho))

if (nrow(sig_pos) > 0) {
  result <- build_gene_expr_matrix(sig_pos, sender_celltype_bins, rec_celltype_bins, bin_ids)
  mat_pos <- result$mat
  sig_pos <- result$sig_df

  # Smooth and z-score
  mat_pos_smooth <- smooth_rows(mat_pos)
  mat_pos_z <- zscore_rows(mat_pos_smooth)

  # Order: group by gene, within each gene order by earliest bin to reach max
  sig_pos$peak_bin <- apply(mat_pos_smooth, 1, function(x) which.max(x))
  sig_pos <- sig_pos %>%
    arrange(gene, peak_bin)

  # Reorder matrix
  mat_pos_z <- mat_pos_z[sig_pos$row_label, , drop = FALSE]

  # Color scale
  col_fun <- colorRamp2(c(-2, 0, 2), c("#4575b4", "white", "#d73027"))

  # Row split by gene
  row_split <- factor(sig_pos$gene, levels = unique(sig_pos$gene))

  ht_pos <- Heatmap(
    mat_pos_z,
    col = col_fun,
    name = "Z-score",
    column_title = "Genes positively correlated with DC1",
    row_title = "Gene (Cell type)",
    cluster_rows = FALSE,
    cluster_columns = FALSE,
    row_split = row_split,
    row_gap = unit(2, "mm"),
    show_row_names = TRUE,
    row_names_side = "left",
    row_names_gp = gpar(fontsize = 8),
    show_column_names = FALSE,
    rect_gp = gpar(col = "lightgrey", lwd = 0.3),
    na_col = "grey90",
    column_title_gp = gpar(fontsize = 12, fontface = "bold")
  )

  draw(ht_pos)
  decorate_heatmap_body("Z-score", {
    x_pos <- bin18_idx / ncol(mat_pos_z)
    grid.lines(x = unit(x_pos, "npc"), y = unit(c(0, 1), "npc"), gp = gpar(col = "black", lwd = 2))
  })

  # Save
  n_rows <- nrow(mat_pos_z)
  fig_height <- max(6, n_rows * 0.25 + 2)
  pdf(file.path(FIG_DIR, "gene_dc1_cor_heatmap_positive.pdf"), width = 8, height = fig_height)
  draw(ht_pos)
  decorate_heatmap_body("Z-score", {
    x_pos <- bin18_idx / ncol(mat_pos_z)
    grid.lines(x = unit(x_pos, "npc"), y = unit(c(0, 1), "npc"), gp = gpar(col = "black", lwd = 2))
  })
  dev.off()
  cat("Saved positive correlation heatmap\n")
} else {
  cat("No positive correlations to plot.\n")
}
```

```{r gene_dc1_cor_heatmap_negative, fig.width=8, fig.height=10}
# Negative correlations heatmap
sig_neg <- gene_dc1_cor_sig %>%
  filter(spearman_rho < 0) %>%
  dplyr::filter(gene_type == 'ligand' & !(is_chemokine)) %>%
  dplyr::filter(pct_expressing > 0.05 | gene == 'Ifn_I') %>%
  dplyr::filter(!(cell_type %in% celltype_rm)) %>%
  arrange(gene, spearman_rho)

if (nrow(sig_neg) > 0) {
  result <- build_gene_expr_matrix(sig_neg, sender_celltype_bins, rec_celltype_bins, bin_ids)
  mat_neg <- result$mat
  sig_neg <- result$sig_df

  # Smooth and z-score
  mat_neg_smooth <- smooth_rows(mat_neg)
  mat_neg_z <- zscore_rows(mat_neg_smooth)

  # Order: group by gene, within each gene order by earliest bin to reach min
  sig_neg$trough_bin <- apply(mat_neg_smooth, 1, function(x) which.min(x))
  sig_neg <- sig_neg %>%
    arrange(gene, trough_bin)

  # Reorder matrix
  mat_neg_z <- mat_neg_z[sig_neg$row_label, , drop = FALSE]

  # Color scale
  col_fun <- colorRamp2(c(-2, 0, 2), c("#4575b4", "white", "#d73027"))

  # Row split by gene
  row_split <- factor(sig_neg$gene, levels = unique(sig_neg$gene))

  ht_neg <- Heatmap(
    mat_neg_z,
    col = col_fun,
    name = "Z-score",
    column_title = "Genes negatively correlated with DC1",
    row_title = "Gene (Cell type)",
    cluster_rows = FALSE,
    cluster_columns = FALSE,
    row_split = row_split,
    row_gap = unit(2, "mm"),
    show_row_names = TRUE,
    row_names_side = "left",
    row_names_gp = gpar(fontsize = 8),
    show_column_names = FALSE,
    rect_gp = gpar(col = "lightgrey", lwd = 0.3),
    na_col = "grey90",
    column_title_gp = gpar(fontsize = 12, fontface = "bold")
  )

  draw(ht_neg)
  decorate_heatmap_body("Z-score", {
    x_pos <- bin18_idx / ncol(mat_neg_z)
    grid.lines(x = unit(x_pos, "npc"), y = unit(c(0, 1), "npc"), gp = gpar(col = "black", lwd = 2))
  })

  # Save
  n_rows <- nrow(mat_neg_z)
  fig_height <- max(6, n_rows * 0.25 + 2)
  pdf(file.path(FIG_DIR, "gene_dc1_cor_heatmap_negative.pdf"), width = 8, height = fig_height)
  draw(ht_neg)
  decorate_heatmap_body("Z-score", {
    x_pos <- bin18_idx / ncol(mat_neg_z)
    grid.lines(x = unit(x_pos, "npc"), y = unit(c(0, 1), "npc"), gp = gpar(col = "black", lwd = 2))
  })
  dev.off()
  cat("Saved negative correlation heatmap\n")
} else {
  cat("No negative correlations to plot.\n")
}
```


---

# Cell Type Feature Frequency by DC Group

This section computes the frequency of cells positive for specific features within each cell type, stratified by DC1 bin groups (high vs low). Niches are pooled by `sample_name + condition_num + dc_group`. Related to analysis for figure S8C. 

```{r celltype_feature_freq_params}
# === Adjustable parameters ===
EXPR_DETECT_THRESHOLD <- 1     # Expression threshold for detecting positive cells (>threshold)
DC_LOW_BINS           <- 3:13  # Bins assigned to "low" DC group
DC_HIGH_BINS          <- 17:27 # Bins assigned to "high" DC group
# Note: bins 1-2, 14-16, and 28+ are excluded from analysis

# Features to test: use all LR genes that exist in obs_df
FEATURES_TO_TEST = adata$var_names
cat("Testing", length(FEATURES_TO_TEST), "features\n")
cat("DC low bins:", min(DC_LOW_BINS), "-", max(DC_LOW_BINS), "\n")
cat("DC high bins:", min(DC_HIGH_BINS), "-", max(DC_HIGH_BINS), "\n")
cat("Expression detection threshold: >", EXPR_DETECT_THRESHOLD, "\n")
```

```{r compute_ct_feature_freq}
# create new obs_df to measure this
exp_df = adata$layers[['counts_segger']] %>% as.matrix() %>% as.data.frame()
colnames(exp_df) = adata$var_names
obs_df = adata$obs
obs_df = cbind(obs_df, exp_df)
obs_df$DC_1_rank = ifelse(is.na(obs_df$DC_1_rank), obs_df$DC_1_rank_env, obs_df$DC_1_rank)

## bin assignment
DC1_N_BINS    <- 30L
DC1_TRIM_LOW  <- 0L
DC1_TRIM_HIGH <- 2L

obs_df$DC1_bin30 <- cut(obs_df$DC_1_rank, breaks = DC1_N_BINS, labels = FALSE)
bin_breaks <- seq(min(obs_df$DC_1_rank, na.rm = TRUE),
                  max(obs_df$DC_1_rank, na.rm = TRUE),
                  length.out = DC1_N_BINS + 1)
bin_lines  <- bin_breaks[(DC1_TRIM_LOW + 1):(DC1_N_BINS - DC1_TRIM_HIGH)]

obs_df <- obs_df[
  !is.na(obs_df$DC1_bin30) &
  obs_df$DC1_bin30 > DC1_TRIM_LOW &
  obs_df$DC1_bin30 <= (DC1_N_BINS - DC1_TRIM_HIGH), ]

DC1_T_MIN <- min(obs_df$DC_1_rank, na.rm = TRUE)
DC1_T_MAX <- max(obs_df$DC_1_rank, na.rm = TRUE)

# Assign cells to DC groups based on their DC1_bin30
# Bins in DC_LOW_BINS -> "low", bins in DC_HIGH_BINS -> "high", others -> NA (excluded)
obs_df$dc_group <- case_when(
  obs_df$DC1_bin30 %in% DC_LOW_BINS  ~ "low",
  obs_df$DC1_bin30 %in% DC_HIGH_BINS ~ "high",
  TRUE ~ NA_character_
)
cat("Cells in low DC group:", sum(obs_df$dc_group == "low", na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
cat("Cells in high DC group:", sum(obs_df$dc_group == "high", na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
cat("Cells excluded (middle/edge bins):", sum(is.na(obs_df$dc_group)), "\n")

# Compute frequencies: for each sample_name + condition_num + dc_group + cell_type,
# calculate fraction of cells positive for each feature

# First, get the unique niche-group combinations with cell counts
niche_group_ct <- obs_df %>%
  filter(!is.na(cell_annotation), !is.na(dc_group), !is.na(sample_name), !is.na(condition_num)) %>%
  group_by(sample_name, condition_num, dc_group, cell_annotation) %>%
  summarize(n_cells = n(), .groups = "drop")

cat("Unique niche-group-celltype combinations:", nrow(niche_group_ct), "\n")
cat("Niches in low DC group:", length(unique(paste(niche_group_ct$sample_name[niche_group_ct$dc_group == "low"],
                                                    niche_group_ct$condition_num[niche_group_ct$dc_group == "low"]))), "\n")
cat("Niches in high DC group:", length(unique(paste(niche_group_ct$sample_name[niche_group_ct$dc_group == "high"],
                                                     niche_group_ct$condition_num[niche_group_ct$dc_group == "high"]))), "\n")

# Compute feature frequencies for each niche-group-celltype combination
# Frequency and offset are based on TOTAL cells in niche (all cell types), not just cells of that type
compute_feature_freqs <- function(df, features, threshold) {
  df_sub <- df %>%
    filter(!is.na(cell_annotation), !is.na(dc_group), !is.na(sample_name), !is.na(condition_num))

  # Get features that exist in data
  features <- intersect(features, names(df_sub))

  # Create binary expression matrix (cells × features)
  expr_mat <- as.matrix(df_sub[, features, drop = FALSE]) > threshold

  # Create niche-group ID (sample_name + condition_num + dc_group) - for total cell counts
  df_sub$niche_group <- paste(df_sub$sample_name, df_sub$condition_num, df_sub$dc_group, sep = "___")

  # Create niche-group-celltype ID - for positive cell counts
  df_sub$niche_group_ct <- paste(df_sub$niche_group, df_sub$cell_annotation, sep = "___")

  # Compute TOTAL cells per niche-group (all cell types)
  niche_total_cells <- df_sub %>%
    group_by(niche_group) %>%
    summarize(n_total_niche = n(), .groups = "drop")

  # Aggregate: sum positive cells per niche-group-celltype
  group_ids <- factor(df_sub$niche_group_ct)
  group_indicator <- model.matrix(~ group_ids - 1)
  colnames(group_indicator) <- levels(group_ids)

  n_pos_mat <- t(group_indicator) %*% expr_mat  # groups × features
  n_cells_ct_tbl <- table(group_ids)  # cells of this type in this niche-group

  # Convert to long format
  result <- expand.grid(
    niche_group_ct = rownames(n_pos_mat),
    feature = colnames(n_pos_mat),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  result$n_positive <- as.vector(n_pos_mat)
  result$n_cells_ct <- as.integer(n_cells_ct_tbl[result$niche_group_ct])

  # Parse niche_group_ct back to components
  parts <- strsplit(result$niche_group_ct, "___")
  result$sample_name <- sapply(parts, `[`, 1)
  result$condition_num <- as.integer(sapply(parts, `[`, 2))
  result$dc_group <- sapply(parts, `[`, 3)
  result$cell_type <- sapply(parts, `[`, 4)
  result$niche_group <- paste(result$sample_name, result$condition_num, result$dc_group, sep = "___")

  # Join total niche cells (all cell types)
  result <- result %>%
    left_join(niche_total_cells, by = "niche_group")

  # Frequency is positive cells of this type / total cells in niche (all types)
  result$freq_positive <- result$n_positive / result$n_total_niche

  result <- result %>%
    select(sample_name, condition_num, dc_group, cell_type, feature,
           n_positive, n_cells_ct, n_total_niche, freq_positive)

  result
}

ct_feature_freq_unfiltered <- compute_feature_freqs(obs_df, FEATURES_TO_TEST, EXPR_DETECT_THRESHOLD)
cat("Total frequency observations:", nrow(ct_feature_freq_unfiltered), "\n")

# Filter to cell types with minimum cells of that type (for Wilcoxon/edgeR per-niche tests)
ct_feature_freq <- ct_feature_freq_unfiltered %>%
  filter(n_cells_ct >= 10)
cat("After filtering (n_cells_ct >= 10):", nrow(ct_feature_freq), "\n")
```

## fisher tests


```{r test_differential_freq_fisher}
# Fisher's exact test for each cell_type × feature combination
# Uses pooled counts across all niches to build 2x2 contingency table
# Uses UNFILTERED data to include sparse cell types like NK_ILC1

MIN_POOLED_CELLS <- 20  # Minimum total cells of that type (pooled across all niches) per group

run_fisher_tests <- function(freq_df, min_cells = MIN_POOLED_CELLS) {
  # Get unique cell_type × feature combinations
  combos <- freq_df %>%
    distinct(cell_type, feature)

  results <- list()

  for (i in seq_len(nrow(combos))) {
    ct <- combos$cell_type[i]
    feat <- combos$feature[i]

    sub <- freq_df %>%
      filter(cell_type == ct, feature == feat)

    # Pool counts across all niches
    n_pos_low <- sum(sub$n_positive[sub$dc_group == "low"], na.rm = TRUE)
    n_pos_high <- sum(sub$n_positive[sub$dc_group == "high"], na.rm = TRUE)
    n_total_low <- sum(sub$n_total_niche[sub$dc_group == "low"], na.rm = TRUE)
    n_total_high <- sum(sub$n_total_niche[sub$dc_group == "high"], na.rm = TRUE)

    # Pool cells of this type across niches
    n_cells_ct_low <- sum(sub$n_cells_ct[sub$dc_group == "low"], na.rm = TRUE)
    n_cells_ct_high <- sum(sub$n_cells_ct[sub$dc_group == "high"], na.rm = TRUE)

    # Filter: need minimum pooled cells of this type in each group
    if (n_cells_ct_low < min_cells || n_cells_ct_high < min_cells) next

    # Need positive totals
    if (n_total_low == 0 || n_total_high == 0) next

    # Build 2x2 contingency table
    # Rows: low, high; Columns: positive, negative
    n_neg_low <- n_total_low - n_pos_low
    n_neg_high <- n_total_high - n_pos_high

    cont_table <- matrix(
      c(n_pos_low, n_neg_low,
        n_pos_high, n_neg_high),
      nrow = 2, byrow = TRUE,
      dimnames = list(c("low", "high"), c("positive", "negative"))
    )

    # Fisher's exact test
    ft <- tryCatch(
      fisher.test(cont_table),
      error = function(e) NULL
    )
    if (is.null(ft)) next

    # Compute odds ratio with Haldane-Anscombe correction (add 0.5 to avoid Inf)
    # OR = (n_pos_high * n_neg_low) / (n_neg_high * n_pos_low)
    # OR > 1 means feature is enriched in HIGH DC group (classical-like)
    # OR < 1 means feature is enriched in LOW DC group (basal-like)
    odds_ratio_corrected <- ((n_pos_high + 0.5) * (n_neg_low + 0.5)) /
                            ((n_neg_high + 0.5) * (n_pos_low + 0.5))

    results[[length(results) + 1]] <- data.frame(
      cell_type = ct,
      feature = feat,
      n_cells_ct_low = n_cells_ct_low,
      n_cells_ct_high = n_cells_ct_high,
      n_pos_low = n_pos_low,
      n_pos_high = n_pos_high,
      n_neg_low = n_neg_low,
      n_neg_high = n_neg_high,
      n_total_niche_low = n_total_low,
      n_total_niche_high = n_total_high,
      freq_low = n_pos_low / n_total_low,
      freq_high = n_pos_high / n_total_high,
      odds_ratio_raw = ft$estimate,
      odds_ratio = odds_ratio_corrected,  # Haldane-Anscombe corrected
      pval = ft$p.value,
      conf_low = ft$conf.int[1],
      conf_high = ft$conf.int[2],
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  }

  if (length(results) == 0) return(data.frame())

  result_df <- do.call(rbind, results)
  result_df$padj <- p.adjust(result_df$pval, method = "BH")
  result_df$log2_odds_ratio <- log2(result_df$odds_ratio)  # uses corrected OR
  result_df$lineage <- LINEAGE_MAP[result_df$cell_type]
  result_df <- result_df[order(result_df$padj), ]

  result_df
}

# Use UNFILTERED data for Fisher test (sparse cell types are handled by pooled-count filter)
fisher_results <- run_fisher_tests(ct_feature_freq_unfiltered)
cat("Fisher tests performed:", nrow(fisher_results), "\n")
cat("Significant (padj < 0.1):", sum(fisher_results$padj < 0.1, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
cat("Significant (padj < 0.05):", sum(fisher_results$padj < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")

# Show top results (odds_ratio > 1 = higher in high DC)
cat("\n=== Fisher: Top features ENRICHED in high DC (late ablation) ===\n")
print(head(fisher_results[fisher_results$odds_ratio > 1, ], 20))

cat("\n=== Fisher: Top features DEPLETED in high DC (late ablation) ===\n")
print(head(fisher_results[fisher_results$odds_ratio < 1, ], 20))
```



## plot fisher tests


```{r barplot_fisher_effector_genes, fig.width=5, fig.height=4}
# Horizontal bar plots of effector genes showing odds ratio, shaded by significance

# Genes to plot
EFFECTOR_GENES_BASE <- c("Stat1", "Prf1", "Gzmb", "Gzmc", "Ifng", "Gzma", "Gzmk", "Fasl", "Tnfsf10")
EFFECTOR_GENES_TCONV <- c(EFFECTOR_GENES_BASE, "Tbx21")

for (ct in VOLCANO_CELLTYPES) {
  # Select genes based on cell type
  genes_to_plot <- if (ct == "Tconv") EFFECTOR_GENES_TCONV else EFFECTOR_GENES_BASE

  # Filter to this cell type and genes of interest
  plot_df <- fisher_results %>%
    filter(cell_type == ct, feature %in% genes_to_plot & padj < 0.05) %>%
    mutate(
      significant = padj < PADJ_THRESH,
      sig_label = case_when(
        padj < 0.001 ~ "***",
        padj < 0.01 ~ "**",
        padj < 0.05 ~ "*",
        TRUE ~ ""
      )
    ) 

  if (nrow(plot_df) == 0) {
    cat("No data for cell type:", ct, "\n")
    next
  }

  # Order genes by padj (most significant at top)
  # In ggplot horizontal bars, top = last factor level, so arrange desc(padj) then set levels
  plot_df <- plot_df %>%
    arrange(desc(padj)) %>%
    mutate(feature = factor(feature, levels = feature))

  # Create horizontal bar plot
  p <- ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = log2_odds_ratio, y = feature)) + #, fill = -log10(padj))) +
    geom_col(width = 0.7) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "solid", color = "black", linewidth = 0.5) +
    geom_text(aes(label = sig_label,
                  x = log2_odds_ratio + sign(log2_odds_ratio) * 0.02),
              hjust = ifelse(plot_df$log2_odds_ratio > 0, 0, 1),
              size = 4, color = "black") +
    # scale_fill_gradient2(
    #   low = "grey70", mid = "steelblue", high = "darkred",
    #   midpoint = -log10(0.05),
    #   name = "-log10(padj)"
    # ) +
    scale_x_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0.1, 0.15))) +
    labs(
      x = "log2(Odds Ratio)",
      y = NULL,
      title = paste0(ct, " — Effector gene positivity")
    ) +
    theme_tufte() +
    rose_theme +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
      legend.position = "right",
      plot.margin = margin(5, 15, 5, 5)
    )

  print(p)

  # Save
  fig_height <- max(3, length(genes_to_plot) * 0.4 + 1)
  ggsave(file.path(FIG_DIR, paste0("barplot_fisher_effector_", ct, "_", file_suffix, ".svg")),
         p, width = 5, height = fig_height)
  ggsave(file.path(FIG_DIR, paste0("barplot_fisher_effector_", ct, "_", file_suffix, ".pdf")),
         p, width = 5, height = fig_height)
  cat("Saved barplot for:", ct, "\n")
}
```


---

# Tier 2: Spatial Connectivities (60μm radius) Per-Sample Consistency

This section tests whether LR associations are consistent across samples using spatial connectivities (60μm radius neighbors). For each sample, we compute Spearman correlations between niche ligand expression and receptor+ fraction, then filter cell type mode results to those associations that replicate across samples.

## Load Spatial Connectivity Data

```{r load_spatial_niche_data}
library(data.table)

# Load pseudobulk expression per cell type (ligand)
niche_lig_sp_df <- arrow::read_parquet(
  file.path(PRECOMPUTED_DIR,
            "st_data_classical_niche_DC_env_pseudobulk_celltype_expression_spatialConn.parquet")
)
setDT(niche_lig_sp_df)

# Load gene positivity per cell type (receptor)
niche_pos_sp_df <- arrow::read_parquet(
  file.path(PRECOMPUTED_DIR,
            "st_data_classical_niche_DC_env_gene_positivity_spatialConn.parquet")
)
setDT(niche_pos_sp_df)

cat("Loaded spatial connectivity data:\n")
cat("  Ligand expression rows:", nrow(niche_lig_sp_df), "\n")
cat("  Receptor positivity rows:", nrow(niche_pos_sp_df), "\n")
```

## Per-Sample Spearman Correlation Testing

```{r}
# define sender and receiver cell types. 

counts_per_celltype = obs_df$cell_annotation %>% table() %>% sort()
SENDER_CELLTYPES = names(counts_per_celltype)[counts_per_celltype > 500]
RECEIVER_CELLTYPES = names(counts_per_celltype)[counts_per_celltype > 500]
SENDER_CELLTYPES
```


```{r tier2_sp_per_sample, warning=FALSE, error=FALSE}
cat("Running per-sample Tier 2 correlation tests (spatial connectivities)...\n")

# Get unique samples
samples <- unique(obs_df$sample_name[!is.na(obs_df$sample_name)])
cat("Samples:", length(samples), "\n")
print(samples)

# Filter to cancer cells (in_trajectory)
cancer_barcodes_all <- obs_df$barcode[obs_df$in_trajectory]

# Get LR genes
ligand_genes <- unique(LR_PAIRS$ligand)
receptor_genes <- unique(LR_PAIRS$receptor_gene)

tier2_sp_sample_results <- list()

for (samp in samples) {
  cat("\nProcessing sample:", samp, "\n")

  # Filter to cancer cells in this sample
  sample_cancer_df <- obs_df %>%
    filter(in_trajectory, sample_name == samp)

  sample_barcodes <- sample_cancer_df$barcode
  n_sample <- length(sample_barcodes)

  if (n_sample < 100) {
    cat("  Skipping - only", n_sample, "cancer cells\n")
    next
  }
  cat("  Cancer cells:", n_sample, "\n")

  # Filter parquet data to this sample's cancer cells
  niche_pos_sp_samp <- niche_pos_sp_df[barcode %in% sample_barcodes]
  niche_lig_sp_samp <- niche_lig_sp_df[barcode %in% sample_barcodes]

  # Pre-index barcode positions
  sample_barcode_idx <- setNames(seq_along(sample_barcodes), sample_barcodes)

  # Loop through cell type pairs
  for (sender_ct in SENDER_CELLTYPES) {
    # Subset ligand expression for this sender cell type
    lig_ct_subset <- niche_lig_sp_samp[cell_type == sender_ct & gene %in% ligand_genes]

    if (nrow(lig_ct_subset) == 0) next

    # Pivot to matrix
    lig_mat <- matrix(0, nrow = n_sample, ncol = length(ligand_genes),
                      dimnames = list(sample_barcodes, ligand_genes))
    for (g in intersect(ligand_genes, unique(lig_ct_subset$gene))) {
      g_subset <- lig_ct_subset[gene == g]
      lig_mat[sample_barcode_idx[g_subset$barcode], g] <- g_subset$value
    }

    for (receiver_ct in RECEIVER_CELLTYPES) {
      # Subset receptor fraction for this receiver cell type
      rec_ct_subset <- niche_pos_sp_samp[cell_type == receiver_ct & gene %in% receptor_genes]

      if (nrow(rec_ct_subset) == 0) next

      # Pivot to matrix
      rec_mat <- matrix(0, nrow = n_sample, ncol = length(receptor_genes),
                        dimnames = list(sample_barcodes, receptor_genes))
      for (g in intersect(receptor_genes, unique(rec_ct_subset$gene))) {
        g_subset <- rec_ct_subset[gene == g]
        rec_mat[sample_barcode_idx[g_subset$barcode], g] <- g_subset$fraction
      }

      # Loop through LR pairs
      for (i in seq_len(nrow(LR_PAIRS))) {
        lig <- LR_PAIRS$ligand[i]
        rec <- LR_PAIRS$receptor_gene[i]

        if (!lig %in% colnames(lig_mat) || !rec %in% colnames(rec_mat)) next

        lig_vec <- lig_mat[, lig]
        rec_vec <- rec_mat[, rec]

        n_lig_nonzero <- sum(lig_vec > 0)
        n_rec_nonzero <- sum(rec_vec > 0)

        # Require at least 30 non-zero for per-sample (relaxed threshold)
        if (n_lig_nonzero < 30 || n_rec_nonzero < 30) {
          next
        }

        # Correlate
        cor_spearman <- cor.test(log1p(lig_vec), rec_vec, method = "spearman")

        tier2_sp_sample_results[[length(tier2_sp_sample_results) + 1]] <- data.frame(
          sample_name = samp,
          sender_celltype = sender_ct,
          receiver_celltype = receiver_ct,
          ligand = lig,
          receptor = rec,
          rho_spearman = cor_spearman$estimate,
          pval_spearman = cor_spearman$p.value,
          n_cells = n_sample,
          n_lig_nonzero = n_lig_nonzero,
          n_rec_nonzero = n_rec_nonzero,
          stringsAsFactors = FALSE
        )
      }
    }
  }
}

tier2_sp_sample_df <- bind_rows(tier2_sp_sample_results)
rownames(tier2_sp_sample_df) <- NULL

cat("\n=== Per-Sample Results ===\n")
cat("Total tests:", nrow(tier2_sp_sample_df), "\n")
cat("Tests with results:", sum(!is.na(tier2_sp_sample_df$rho_spearman)), "\n")
```




## Compute Sample Consistency Metrics

```{r tier2_sp_sample_consistency}
# Summarize consistency across samples for each LR × cell type combination
cor_threshold <- 0.1

consistency_df <- tier2_sp_sample_df %>%
  filter(!is.na(rho_spearman)) %>%
  group_by(sender_celltype, receiver_celltype, ligand, receptor) %>%
  summarize(
    n_samples = n(),
    n_positive = sum(rho_spearman > cor_threshold, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_sig_positive = sum(rho_spearman > cor_threshold & pval_spearman < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_negative = sum(rho_spearman < -cor_threshold, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_sig_negative = sum(rho_spearman < -cor_threshold & pval_spearman < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_rho_spearman = mean(rho_spearman, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_rho_spearman = sd(rho_spearman, na.rm = TRUE),
    min_rho_spearman = min(rho_spearman, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_rho_spearman = max(rho_spearman, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    frac_positive = n_positive / n_samples,
    frac_sig_positive = n_sig_positive / n_samples,
    consistent = frac_positive >= 0.75 | frac_positive <= 0.25  # >75% agree on direction
  )

cat("\n=== Consistency Summary ===\n")
cat("LR × cell type combinations tested in >=2 samples:", sum(consistency_df$n_samples >= 2), "\n")
cat("Consistent direction (>=75% agree):", sum(consistency_df$consistent & consistency_df$n_samples >= 2), "\n")
cat("Consistently positive (>=75% samples positive):",
    sum(consistency_df$frac_positive >= 0.75 & consistency_df$n_samples >= 2), "\n")
cat("Consistently sig positive (>=75% samples sig positive):",
    sum(consistency_df$frac_sig_positive >= 0.75 & consistency_df$n_samples >= 2), "\n")
```

## Per-Sample Consistency Heatmap

```{r tier2_sp_sample_heatmap, fig.width=10, fig.height=8}
# Heatmap of per-sample correlations for top consistent LR pairs
top_consistent <- consistency_df %>%
  filter(n_samples >= 3, frac_positive >= 0.75, mean_rho_spearman > 0.05) %>%
  arrange(desc(mean_rho_spearman)) %>%
  head(30)

if (nrow(top_consistent) > 0) {
  # Get per-sample rhos for these pairs
  top_sample_data <- tier2_sp_sample_df %>%
    inner_join(top_consistent %>% select(sender_celltype, receiver_celltype, ligand, receptor),
               by = c("sender_celltype", "receiver_celltype", "ligand", "receptor")) %>%
    mutate(lr_label = paste0(ligand, "→", receptor, " (", sender_celltype, "→", receiver_celltype, ")"))

  # Pivot to matrix
  sample_mat <- top_sample_data %>%
    select(lr_label, sample_name, rho_spearman) %>%
    pivot_wider(names_from = sample_name, values_from = rho_spearman) %>%
    tibble::column_to_rownames("lr_label") %>%
    as.matrix()

  # Plot heatmap
  col_fun <- colorRamp2(c(-0.3, 0, 0.3), c("#4575b4", "white", "#d73027"))

  ht <- Heatmap(
    sample_mat,
    col = col_fun,
    name = "Spearman ρ",
    cluster_rows = TRUE,
    cluster_columns = TRUE,
    show_row_names = TRUE,
    row_names_gp = gpar(fontsize = 7),
    show_column_names = TRUE,
    column_names_gp = gpar(fontsize = 9),
    column_title = "Per-Sample LR Colocalization (Spatial 60μm)",
    na_col = "grey90",
    rect_gp = gpar(col = "lightgrey", lwd = 0.3)
  )

  draw(ht)

  # Save
  svg(file.path(FIG_DIR, "tier2_sp_per_sample_consistency_heatmap.svg"), width = 10, height = 8)
  draw(ht)
  dev.off()
  cat("Saved per-sample consistency heatmap\n")
}
```

## Save Per-Sample Results

```{r save_per_sample_results}
# Save per-sample results
write.csv(tier2_sp_sample_df, file.path(PRECOMPUTED_DIR, "lr_dc1_tier2_sp_per_sample.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(consistency_df, file.path(PRECOMPUTED_DIR, "lr_dc1_tier2_sp_sample_consistency.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
cat("Saved per-sample results to:", PRECOMPUTED_DIR, "\n")
```

---

# Join Spatial Consistency to cell type mode Results

Join the per-sample consistency metrics from spatial connectivities to cell type mode LM results for filtering.

## Load Data (if necessary)

```{r load_ctmode_and_consistency, eval=FALSE}
# Uncomment to load if necessary
lm_data <- readRDS(file.path(PRECOMPUTED_DIR, "lr_lm_results.rds"))
lm_df_ctmode <- lm_data$lm_df_ctmode
consistency_df <- readr::read_csv(file.path(PRECOMPUTED_DIR, "lr_dc1_tier2_sp_sample_consistency.csv"))

# Save for diagnostics
ligand_metrics = readr::read_csv(file.path(FIG_DIR, "ligand_celltype_filtering_metrics.csv"))
receptor_metrics = readr::read_csv(file.path(FIG_DIR, "receptor_celltype_filtering_metrics.csv"))

# join to cell type mode and 3 df
lm_df_ctmode = left_join(lm_df_ctmode, ligand_metrics %>% dplyr::select(-lineage), by = c('sender_celltype' = 'cell_type', 'ligand' = 'gene'))
lm_df_ctmode = left_join(lm_df_ctmode, receptor_metrics %>% dplyr::select(-lineage), by = c('receiver_celltype' = 'cell_type', 'receptor' = 'gene'), suffix = c('.ligand', '.receptor'))

```



## Join Consistency to cell type mode Results

```{r join_consistency_to_ctmode}
# Join consistency_df to lm_df_ctmode
lm_df_ctmode_with_consistency <- lm_df_ctmode %>%
  left_join(
    consistency_df %>% select(sender_celltype, receiver_celltype, ligand, receptor,
                               n_samples, n_positive, n_sig_positive, #n_negative, n_sig_negative,
                               frac_positive, frac_sig_positive,
                               mean_rho_spearman, sd_rho_spearman, consistent),
    by = c("sender_celltype", "receiver_celltype", "ligand", "receptor")
  )

cat("Joined consistency metrics to lm_df_ctmode\n")
cat("Total associations:", nrow(lm_df_ctmode_with_consistency), "\n")
cat("With consistency data:", sum(!is.na(lm_df_ctmode_with_consistency$n_samples)), "\n")
cat("Number of ligands tested:", length(unique(lm_df_ctmode_with_consistency$ligand)), "\n")
cat("Number of receptors tested:", length(unique(lm_df_ctmode_with_consistency$receptor)), "\n")
cat("Number of cell types tested:", length(unique(lm_df_ctmode_with_consistency$sender_celltype)), "\n")
```



## Apply Consistency Filter

```{r tier1_vs_consistency_comparison}
# Define filtering criteria
TIER1_PADJ <- 0.05
TIER1_SLOPE_MIN <- 0
TIER1_PCT_LIG_MIN <- 0.05
TIER1_PCT_REC_MIN <- 0.05
MIN_N_POSITIVE <- 2

# Get ligand/receptor expression metrics from lm_df_ctmode if available
# Check if pct_expressing columns exist
has_pct_cols <- "pct_expressing.ligand" %in% names(lm_df_ctmode_with_consistency) ||
                "pct_expressing" %in% names(lm_df_ctmode_with_consistency)

# Identify Tier 1 significant associations
# Exceptions that bypass expression filter:
# 1. Mif-Cxcr4 Classical->Macrophage (just below threshold)
# 2. NK_ILC1 receiver_lineage with decent correlation (few cells = low expression %)
if (has_pct_cols && "pct_expressing.ligand" %in% names(lm_df_ctmode_with_consistency)) {
  tier1_sig <- lm_df_ctmode_with_consistency %>%
    dplyr::filter(
      (padj < TIER1_PADJ & slope > TIER1_SLOPE_MIN &
       pct_expressing.ligand > TIER1_PCT_LIG_MIN &
       pct_expressing.receptor > TIER1_PCT_REC_MIN) |
      # Exception 1: Mif-Cxcr4 Classical->Macrophage
      (receptor == "Cxcr4" & receiver_celltype == "Macrophage" &
       ligand == "Mif" & sender_celltype == "Classical" &
       padj < TIER1_PADJ & slope > TIER1_SLOPE_MIN) 
    )
} else {
  # If expression columns don't exist, just filter by padj and slope
  tier1_sig <- lm_df_ctmode_with_consistency %>%
    filter(padj < TIER1_PADJ, slope > TIER1_SLOPE_MIN)
}

cat("\n=== Tier 1 Significant Associations ===\n")
cat("Total Tier 1 significant:", nrow(tier1_sig), "\n")

# Apply consistency filter with exceptions:
# - For slope >= 0: require n_positive >= MIN_N_POSITIVE
# - For slope < 0: require n_sig_negative >= 2
# Exceptions:
# 1. Cxcr4 receptor + Macrophage receiver + Mif ligand + Classical sender (just below threshold)
# 2. NK_ILC1 receiver_lineage: allow if mean_rho_spearman > 0.05 (few cells = low n_positive)
tier1_with_consistency <- tier1_sig %>%
  dplyr::filter(
    # Main filter: direction-appropriate consistency
    (slope > 0 & !is.na(n_positive) & n_positive >= MIN_N_POSITIVE) |
   # (slope < 0 & !is.na(n_sig_negative) & n_sig_negative >= 2) |
    # Exception 1: Mif-Cxcr4 with Classical sender and Macrophage receiver
    (receptor == "Cxcr4" & receiver_celltype == "Macrophage" &
     ligand == "Mif" & sender_celltype == "Classical") |
    # Exception 2: NK_ILC1 receiver with decent correlation
    (receiver_celltype == "NK_ILC1"  & mean_rho_spearman > 0.05)
  )

cat("After consistency filter (with exceptions):", nrow(tier1_with_consistency), "\n")
cat("  Positive slope (n_positive >=", MIN_N_POSITIVE, "):",
    sum(tier1_with_consistency$slope >= 0 & !is.na(tier1_with_consistency$n_positive) &
        tier1_with_consistency$n_positive >= MIN_N_POSITIVE), "\n")
# cat("  Negative slope (n_sig_negative >= 2):",
#     sum(tier1_with_consistency$slope < 0 & !is.na(tier1_with_consistency$n_sig_negative) &
#         tier1_with_consistency$n_sig_negative >= 2), "\n")
cat("  Exceptions retained:\n")
cat("    Mif-Cxcr4 Classical->Macrophage:",
    sum(tier1_with_consistency$receptor == "Cxcr4" &
        tier1_with_consistency$receiver_celltype == "Macrophage" &
        tier1_with_consistency$ligand == "Mif" &
        tier1_with_consistency$sender_celltype == "Classical"), "\n")
cat("    NK_ILC1 receiver (rho > 0.05):",
    sum(tier1_with_consistency$receiver_celltype == "NK_ILC1" &
        !is.na(tier1_with_consistency$mean_rho_spearman) &
        tier1_with_consistency$mean_rho_spearman > 0.05), "\n")

# Identify removed associations (those failing both direction-specific filters)
tier1_removed <- tier1_sig %>%
  filter(
    !((slope >= 0 & !is.na(n_positive) & n_positive >= MIN_N_POSITIVE)) #|
    #  (slope < 0 & !is.na(n_sig_negative) & n_sig_negative >= 2))
  )

cat("\n=== Associations REMOVED by n_positive filter ===\n")
cat("Removed:", nrow(tier1_removed), "associations\n")

if (nrow(tier1_removed) > 0) {
  cat("\nRemoved associations (showing key columns):\n")
  tier1_removed %>%
    dplyr::select(sender_celltype, receiver_celltype, ligand, receptor, pathway,
           slope, padj, n_samples, n_positive, mean_rho_spearman) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(desc(slope)) %>%
    print(n = 50)

  # Breakdown by reason
  cat("\n--- Breakdown of removals ---\n")
  cat("No consistency data (NA):", sum(is.na(tier1_removed$n_positive)), "\n")
  cat("n_positive = 0:", sum(tier1_removed$n_positive == 0, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
  cat("n_positive = 1:", sum(tier1_removed$n_positive == 1, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")

  # Summarize by ligand
  cat("\n--- Removed by ligand ---\n")
  tier1_removed %>%
    dplyr::count(ligand, sort = TRUE) %>%
    head(20) %>%
    print()

  # Summarize by sender-receiver pair
  cat("\n--- Removed by sender→receiver ---\n")
  tier1_removed %>%
    dplyr::count(sender_celltype, receiver_celltype, sort = TRUE) %>%
    head(15) %>%
    print()

  cat("\n--- Removed by sender ---\n")
  tier1_removed %>%
    dplyr::count(sender_celltype, sort = TRUE) %>%
    head(15) %>%
    print()

  cat("\n--- Removed by receiver ---\n")
  tier1_removed %>%
    dplyr::count(receiver_celltype, sort = TRUE) %>%
    head(15) %>%
    print()

  cat("\n--- Distinct LR pairs removed ---\n")
  tier1_removed %>%
    dplyr::select(ligand, receptor) %>%
    distinct() %>%
    nrow() %>%
    print()
}

# Save the filtered results
write.csv(tier1_with_consistency,
          file.path(PRECOMPUTED_DIR, "lr_dc1_tier1_with_spatial_consistency_filtered.csv"),
          row.names = FALSE)
cat("\nSaved filtered results to:", file.path(PRECOMPUTED_DIR, "lr_dc1_tier1_with_spatial_consistency_filtered.csv"), "\n")
```

---

# Ligand Heatmaps (with Spatial Consistency Filter)

Recreate ligand heatmaps using only associations that pass the spatial consistency filter (n_positive >= 2).

## Separate heatmaps by ligand DC1 correlation direction

Filter ligands by their Spearman correlation with DC1 (using the same rho threshold), then create separate heatmaps for ligands positively vs negatively correlated with DC1.

```{r ligand_dc1_correlation_filter}
COR_PADJ_THRESH = 0.01
COR_RHO_THRESH = .7
# Get ligand-DC1 correlations (filter by same rho threshold)
# Keep cell type to join by both ligand AND sender cell type
ligand_dc1_cor <- gene_dc1_cor_df %>%
  filter(gene_type == "ligand",
         padj < COR_PADJ_THRESH,
         abs(spearman_rho) > COR_RHO_THRESH) %>%
  select(gene, cell_type, spearman_rho)

cat("Ligand-cell type pairs passing DC1 correlation filter (|rho| >", COR_RHO_THRESH, "):", nrow(ligand_dc1_cor), "\n")
cat("  DC1+ pairs (rho > 0):", sum(ligand_dc1_cor$spearman_rho > 0), "\n")
cat("  DC1- pairs (rho < 0):", sum(ligand_dc1_cor$spearman_rho < 0), "\n")

# Join to tier1_with_consistency by ligand AND sender_celltype
tier1_with_lig_dc1 <- tier1_with_consistency %>%
  inner_join(ligand_dc1_cor, by = c("ligand" = "gene", "sender_celltype" = "cell_type"))

cat("\nLR pairs after joining by ligand + sender_celltype:", nrow(tier1_with_lig_dc1), "\n")
cat("  With DC1+ ligands:", sum(tier1_with_lig_dc1$spearman_rho > 0), "\n")
cat("  With DC1- ligands:", sum(tier1_with_lig_dc1$spearman_rho < 0), "\n")

cat("\nDC1+ ligand-celltype pairs:\n")
print(tier1_with_lig_dc1 %>% filter(spearman_rho > 0) %>% distinct(ligand, sender_celltype, spearman_rho))
cat("\nDC1- ligand-celltype pairs:\n")
print(tier1_with_lig_dc1 %>% filter(spearman_rho < 0) %>% distinct(ligand, sender_celltype, spearman_rho))
```

```{r ligand_heatmap_dc1_positive_ligands, fig.width=12, fig.height=30}
# Heatmaps for DC1-POSITIVE correlated LIGANDS (matched by sender cell type)

sig_dc1_pos_lig <- tier1_with_lig_dc1 %>%
  dplyr::filter(spearman_rho > 0)

if (nrow(sig_dc1_pos_lig) > 0) {
  cat("Plotting associations for DC1+ ligands:", nrow(sig_dc1_pos_lig), "pairs\n")
  cat("Ligand-celltype pairs:", paste(unique(paste0(sig_dc1_pos_lig$ligand, " (", sig_dc1_pos_lig$sender_celltype, ")")), collapse = ", "), "\n")

  plot_lr_heatmap_celltype(
    sig_df = sig_dc1_pos_lig,
    sender_celltype_bins = sender_celltype_bins,
    rec_celltype_bins = rec_celltype_bins,
    cov_bins = cov_bins,
    DC1_N_BINS = DC1_N_BINS,
    title_suffix = paste0("DC1+ Ligands (rho > ", COR_RHO_THRESH, ", n_pos>=2)"),
    file_suffix = "dc1_pos_ligands_consistent",
    FIG_DIR = FIG_DIR,
    x_axis_label = X_LAB, format = 'pdf'
  )
} else {
  cat("No associations for DC1+ ligands passing consistency filter.\n")
}
```

```{r ligand_heatmap_dc1_negative_ligands, fig.width=12, fig.height=20}
# Heatmaps for DC1-NEGATIVE correlated LIGANDS (matched by sender cell type)

sig_dc1_neg_lig <- tier1_with_lig_dc1 %>%
  dplyr::filter(spearman_rho < 0)

if (nrow(sig_dc1_neg_lig) > 0) {
  cat("Plotting associations for DC1- ligands:", nrow(sig_dc1_neg_lig), "pairs\n")
  cat("Ligand-celltype pairs:", paste(unique(paste0(sig_dc1_neg_lig$ligand, " (", sig_dc1_neg_lig$sender_celltype, ")")), collapse = ", "), "\n")

  plot_lr_heatmap_celltype(
    sig_df = sig_dc1_neg_lig,
    sender_celltype_bins = sender_celltype_bins,
    rec_celltype_bins = rec_celltype_bins,
    cov_bins = cov_bins,
    DC1_N_BINS = DC1_N_BINS,
    title_suffix = paste0("DC1- Ligands (rho < -", COR_RHO_THRESH, ", n_pos>=2)"),
    file_suffix = "dc1_neg_ligands_consistent",
    FIG_DIR = FIG_DIR,
    x_axis_label = X_LAB, format = 'pdf'
  )
} else {
  cat("No associations for DC1- ligands passing consistency filter.\n")
}


```

## Chemokine Ligand Heatmaps (with Spatial Consistency Filter)

Plots related to Fig S8A. 

```{r chemokine_ligand_heatmap_consistent, fig.width=12, fig.height=15}
# Filter tier1_with_consistency for chemokine ligands only
chemokine_sig <- tier1_with_consistency %>%
  filter(ligand %in% CHEMOKINE_LIGANDS)

if (nrow(chemokine_sig) > 0) {
  cat("Plotting chemokine ligand associations:", nrow(chemokine_sig), "pairs\n")
  cat("Ligands:", paste(unique(chemokine_sig$ligand), collapse = ", "), "\n")
  cat("Receptors:", paste(unique(chemokine_sig$receptor), collapse = ", "), "\n")

  plot_lr_heatmap_celltype(
    sig_df = chemokine_sig,
    sender_celltype_bins = sender_celltype_bins,
    rec_celltype_bins = rec_celltype_bins,
    cov_bins = cov_bins,
    DC1_N_BINS = DC1_N_BINS,
    title_suffix = "Chemokine Ligands (n_pos>=2)",
    file_suffix = "chemokine_ligands_consistent",
    FIG_DIR = FIG_DIR,
    x_axis_label = X_LAB,  receptor_order = c("Cxcr4", "Cxcr6",  "Cxcr3", "Ccr4", "Ccr2"), format = 'pdf'
  )
} else {
  cat("No chemokine ligand associations passing consistency filter.\n")
}
```

---

```{r session_info}
sessionInfo()
```


---
